OAI-001.title: OpenAI failed to deliver the opt-out tool it promised by 2025
OAI-001.action: OpenAI failed to deliver its promised Media Manager opt-out tool by its self-imposed 2025 deadline.
OAI-001.background: OpenAI was fighting class action lawsuits filed by artists, writers, YouTubers, and news organizations including The New York Times and Radio-Canada, all claiming the startup trained on their works illegally. Plaintiffs included authors Sarah Silverman and Ta-Nehisi Coates. Products like ChatGPT and video generator Sora had been shown to reproduce copyrighted content.

OAI-002.title: OpenAI is losing money on its pricey ChatGPT Pro plan, CEO Sam Altman says
OAI-002.action: OpenAI revealed it is losing money on its $200-per-month ChatGPT Pro plan due to higher-than-expected usage.
OAI-002.background: OpenAI has been facing financial challenges, with expenditures outpacing revenue. The company has been exploring different pricing strategies for its AI products to improve profitability. Despite raising significant capital, OpenAI is not yet profitable and is considering various approaches to increase revenue and attract new investments.

OAI-003.title: OpenAI is turning its attention to ‘superintelligence’
OAI-003.action: OpenAI shifts its focus towards developing superintelligence.
OAI-003.background: OpenAI has been involved in the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI) and has defined it as highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work. The company has been collaborating closely with Microsoft and has been considering the potential impact of AI agents on the workforce and economic output. Despite the limitations of current AI technology, OpenAI is confident in overcoming these challenges and is undergoing corporate restructuring to attract investors.

OAI-004.title: New OpenAI job listings reveal the company’s robotics plans
OAI-004.action: OpenAI revived its robotics department and announced plans to develop its own robots.
OAI-004.background: OpenAI had previously disbanded its robotics department but is now focusing on creating general-purpose, adaptive, and versatile robots that can operate with human-like intelligence in dynamic, real-world settings. The robotics sector is attracting significant investment, and there is growing interest in developing humanoid robots and other advanced robotic systems.

OAI-005.title: How OpenAI’s bot crushed this seven-person company’s website ‘like a DDoS attack’
OAI-005.action: OpenAI deployed its bots to scrape data from a website.
OAI-005.background: A small company experienced a significant increase in server requests, which appeared to be similar to a distributed denial-of-service attack. The company had a large database of detailed 3D image files that were valuable for AI training. The company had not configured its robot.txt file to block certain bots, which left it vulnerable to automated data scraping.

OAI-006.title: OpenAI presents its preferred version of AI regulation in a new ‘blueprint’
OAI-006.action: OpenAI presented an 'economic blueprint' for AI regulation.
OAI-006.background: The U.S. government has largely left AI regulation to the states, leading to a fragmented approach with numerous conflicting AI-related bills. OpenAI has been advocating for more substantive federal action to support AI development and infrastructure. The company has been increasing its lobbying efforts and hiring former government officials to influence AI policy.

OAI-007.title: OpenAI appoints BlackRock exec to its board
OAI-007.action: OpenAI appointed Adebayo Ogunlesi to its board of directors.
OAI-007.background: After OpenAI's board fired CEO Sam Altman in late 2023 and reinstated him days later, Altman demanded a remade board. The reconstituted board, chaired by Bret Taylor, included prominent figures from finance, technology, and national security, with Quora CEO Adam D'Angelo as the sole holdover from the previous board.

OAI-008.title: ChatGPT now lets you schedule reminders and recurring tasks
OAI-008.action: OpenAI launched a new beta feature called 'tasks' for ChatGPT, allowing users to schedule reminders and recurring tasks.
OAI-008.background: OpenAI is exploring the development of AI models that can act independently, known as AI agents. The company is in a beta period to learn how users interact with these capabilities before broader release. This move is part of OpenAI's strategy to enhance the functionality of its AI assistant, ChatGPT, and to prepare for more advanced agentic systems in the future.

OAI-009.title: OpenAI quietly revises policy doc to remove reference to ‘politically unbiased’ AI
OAI-009.action: OpenAI revised its policy document to remove language endorsing 'politically unbiased' AI.
OAI-009.background: There has been ongoing discourse and criticism regarding bias in AI systems, particularly accusations from political figures and industry leaders about AI models censoring certain viewpoints. OpenAI has been part of this conversation, with its AI models like ChatGPT being scrutinized for perceived biases. The company has previously stated that any biases are unintended and are considered bugs.

OAI-010.title: OpenAI is bankrolling Axios’ expansion into four new markets
OAI-010.action: OpenAI funded Axios' expansion into four new cities.
OAI-010.background: OpenAI had signed content-sharing and licensing deals with several publishers including Vox Media, The Atlantic, and the Financial Times, but had never directly funded a media company's newsroom operations. Newsletter-based media organization Axios was looking to expand its local newsletter coverage into new U.S. cities under a three-year partnership.

OAI-011.title: OpenAI tests phone number-only ChatGPT signups
OAI-011.action: OpenAI began testing a feature that allows new ChatGPT users to sign up with only a phone number.
OAI-011.background: OpenAI has been exploring ways to increase user engagement and growth for its ChatGPT product. The company is seeking to make its services more accessible and reduce barriers to entry for potential users. Despite having a large user base and significant revenue expectations, OpenAI is not yet profitable and is considering various strategies to improve its financial performance.

OAI-012.title: ChatGPT’s head of product will testify in the US government’s case against Google
OAI-012.action: OpenAI's head of ChatGPT product, Nick Turley, was confirmed to testify as a witness for the U.S. government in its antitrust case against Google.
OAI-012.background: The U.S. government is pursuing an antitrust case against Google, alleging that Google holds a monopoly in search. As part of this case, the Department of Justice is seeking testimony from various competitors of Google, including OpenAI, to discuss topics such as generative AI's relationship with search access points and barriers to entry.

OAI-013.title: OpenAI is trying to extend human life, with help from a longevity startup
OAI-013.action: OpenAI collaborated with Retro Biosciences to train a new AI model for biological research.
OAI-013.background: Retro Biosciences, a longevity science startup, has been working on extending human lifespan by re-engineering proteins. The company is backed by Sam Altman and has been collaborating with OpenAI for about a year. The research focuses on proteins that can potentially rejuvenate human cells, which is a promising area for developing human organs and replacement cells.

OAI-014.title: AI benchmarking organization criticized for waiting to disclose funding from OpenAI
OAI-014.action: OpenAI funded and gained access to the FrontierMath benchmark developed by Epoch AI.
OAI-014.background: Epoch AI, a nonprofit organization, was developing math benchmarks for AI with funding primarily from Open Philanthropy. The organization faced criticism for not disclosing all sources of funding and access arrangements, which raised concerns about transparency and potential conflicts of interest in the AI community.

OAI-015.title: OpenAI’s agent tool may be nearing release
OAI-015.action: OpenAI developed and is preparing to release an AI tool called Operator.
OAI-015.background: The AI agent market is emerging as a significant segment with potential high value, attracting interest from major tech companies. OpenAI's competitors, such as Anthropic and Google, are also making moves in this space. There are ongoing discussions about the safety and reliability of AI agents, which are considered risky and speculative technologies.

OAI-016.title: Microsoft is no longer OpenAI’s exclusive cloud provider
OAI-016.action: OpenAI entered into a new AI infrastructure deal with SoftBank, Oracle, and others.
OAI-016.background: OpenAI has been facing challenges with compute capacity, which has delayed its product development. There has been tension with Microsoft, its major investor and collaborator, over these capacity issues. OpenAI has been seeking additional cloud computing resources to support its research and training needs.

OAI-017.title: OpenAI teams up with SoftBank and Oracle on $500B data center project
OAI-017.action: OpenAI teamed up with SoftBank and Oracle to build multiple data centers for AI in the U.S.
OAI-017.background: The AI industry is rapidly growing, requiring substantial infrastructure to support AI systems. There is a push for increased investment in data centers in the U.S. to secure American leadership in AI and address the growing demand for compute power. OpenAI has faced challenges due to limited compute availability, which has delayed product development.

OAI-018.title: UK plans ‘digital wallet’ for driver’s licenses and other ID, plus a chatbot powered by OpenAI
OAI-018.action: OpenAI collaborated with the UK government to develop a chatbot for the GOV.UK portal.
OAI-018.background: The UK government is making a significant push towards digital transformation and AI integration in public services. As part of this initiative, they are developing a secure digital wallet for government-issued credentials and exploring AI tools to enhance user interaction with government services. This comes amid broader efforts to build an AI economy and address AI safety concerns.

OAI-019.title: Microsoft’s relationship with OpenAI cracked when it hired Mustafa Suleyman, rival Marc Benioff says
OAI-019.action: OpenAI ended Microsoft's exclusivity as their cloud provider by teaming up with SoftBank and Oracle on a data center project.
OAI-019.background: OpenAI has been experiencing a lack of available compute for product delays and needs more data center capacity. Their exclusive agreement with Microsoft was set to end upon achieving a certain technological milestone. Tensions between OpenAI and Microsoft have been building, partly due to OpenAI's competing enterprise product and personnel dynamics.

OAI-020.title: OpenAI says it may store deleted Operator data for up to 90 days
OAI-020.action: OpenAI implemented a policy to store deleted Operator data for up to 90 days.
OAI-020.background: OpenAI released Operator, a general-purpose AI agent with a built-in browser that captures screenshots to understand how to interact with apps. Operator can automate tasks like booking travel, making reservations, and shopping online. Privacy advocates had been raising concerns about how AI agents handle and retain user data during automated web browsing sessions.

OAI-021.title: OpenAI launches Operator, an AI agent that performs tasks autonomously
OAI-021.action: OpenAI launched a research preview of Operator, an AI agent that performs tasks autonomously.
OAI-021.background: OpenAI has been developing AI agents to automate tasks and take actions on behalf of users. The company has been cautious in its approach due to safety risks associated with AI systems that can take actions on the web. OpenAI aims to ensure that its AI agents are safe and reliable before releasing them to the public. The company has been collaborating with various businesses to ensure compliance with terms of service agreements and has implemented safeguards to prevent misuse.

OAI-022.title: AI companies upped their federal lobbying spend in 2024 amid regulatory uncertainty
OAI-022.action: OpenAI increased its lobbying expenditures to $1.76 million in 2024.
OAI-022.background: In 2024, there was significant regulatory uncertainty surrounding AI at the federal level in the U.S., with a substantial increase in AI-related legislative proposals. Many AI companies, including OpenAI, were actively engaging in lobbying efforts to influence these legislative developments. The environment was marked by a lack of comprehensive federal AI regulation, prompting companies to push for legislation that aligns with their interests.

OAI-023.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT plan for US government agencies
OAI-023.action: OpenAI launched a version of its ChatGPT designed for U.S. government agencies.
OAI-023.background:
There is increasing competition in the AI sector, particularly from China, and U.S. government agencies are seeking advanced AI tools to enhance their operations. OpenAI has previously developed a corporate-focused tier, ChatGPT Enterprise, and there is a demand for secure, compliant AI solutions in government settings.

There is a growing trend among tech companies to offer AI services specifically tailored for U.S. government needs. This trend includes companies like Salesforce and others, who are developing specialized business units or products to serve national security and federal agency requirements. OpenAI is part of this movement, aiming to provide AI solutions to enhance government operations.

OAI-024.title: SoftBank in talks to invest as much as $25B in OpenAI, report says
OAI-024.action: OpenAI engaged in talks with SoftBank for a potential investment of up to $25 billion.
OAI-024.background: The environment includes a competitive AI landscape with new models emerging, such as DeepSeek's R1, which has impacted market perceptions about AI development costs. OpenAI is also navigating its relationship with Microsoft and exploring ways to diversify its funding sources and reduce dependency on a single cloud provider.

OAI-025.title: OpenAI said to be in talks to raise $40B at a $340B valuation
OAI-025.action: OpenAI is in talks to raise up to $40 billion in a funding round.
OAI-025.background: OpenAI was valued at $157 billion in October 2024 and lost around $5 billion against revenues of $3.7 billion in 2024. The company had committed to the Stargate project to build AI data centers across the U.S. in partnership with SoftBank and others, requiring billions of dollars in contributions to get started.

OAI-026.title: OpenAI will offer its tech to US national labs for nuclear weapons research
OAI-026.action: OpenAI plans to let U.S. National Laboratories use its AI models for nuclear weapons security and other scientific projects.
OAI-026.background: The U.S. National Laboratories, part of the Department of Energy's network, are involved in various research and development projects, including those related to nuclear defense. There is a focus on reducing the risk of nuclear war and securing nuclear materials. OpenAI has a partnership with Microsoft, which is its lead investor, and there is an interest in advancing science and strengthening national security through AI.

OAI-027.title: OpenAI used this subreddit to test AI persuasion
OAI-027.action: OpenAI used the subreddit r/ChangeMyView to test the persuasive abilities of its AI reasoning models.
OAI-027.background: OpenAI is focused on developing AI models with strong reasoning and persuasive capabilities. The company is interested in evaluating these models against human performance to ensure they do not become overly persuasive, which could pose risks. OpenAI has been involved in obtaining high-quality datasets for training and testing its AI models, amidst challenges in accessing such data.

OAI-028.title: Sam Altman: OpenAI has been on the ‘wrong side of history’ concerning open source
OAI-028.action: OpenAI considers open sourcing older models and revealing more about their reasoning models.
OAI-028.background: During a Reddit AMA on Jan 31, 2025, OpenAI faced mounting pressure after Chinese lab DeepSeek released its R1 reasoning model with full chain-of-thought visibility, eroding OpenAI's AI lead. CEO Altman admitted DeepSeek lessened their advantage, while OpenAI's closed-source approach drew criticism amid its pursuit of the Stargate data center project and a record financing round.

OAI-029.title: OpenAI launches o3-mini, its latest ‘reasoning’ model
OAI-029.action: OpenAI launched a new AI reasoning model called o3-mini.
OAI-029.background: OpenAI is facing competitive pressure from Chinese companies and is working to strengthen its position in the AI industry. The company is also focusing on improving its relationship with government entities and pursuing large-scale projects. There is a need to offer advanced AI solutions that are both powerful and affordable to maintain its competitive edge.

OAI-030.title: OpenAI unveils a new ChatGPT agent for ‘deep research’
OAI-030.action: OpenAI unveiled a new ChatGPT agent for deep research.
OAI-030.background: OpenAI has been focusing on developing AI tools that assist with complex and in-depth research tasks across various fields such as finance, science, policy, and engineering. The company aims to provide more thorough, precise, and reliable research capabilities to users who require detailed information from multiple sources. This initiative is part of OpenAI's broader strategy to enhance the functionality and accuracy of its AI models, particularly in scenarios where precise and well-documented outputs are crucial.

OAI-031.title: OpenAI’s new trademark application hints at humanoid robots, smart jewelry, and more
OAI-031.action: OpenAI filed a trademark application with the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office.
OAI-031.background: OpenAI has been exploring various new technologies and product lines, including hardware and AI-powered consumer devices. The company has been collaborating with notable designers and hiring for new teams to expand its capabilities in robotics and custom chip development. There is industry speculation about OpenAI's future plans in these areas, and the company is positioning itself to potentially leverage advancements in quantum computing to enhance AI model performance.

OAI-032.title: OpenAI’s Operator agent helped me move, but I had to help it, too
OAI-032.action: OpenAI developed and launched the AI agent Operator.
OAI-032.background: The tech industry is striving to create AI systems that can automate mundane tasks, allowing users to focus on more meaningful activities. However, fully autonomous AI systems remain elusive due to challenges like decision-making reliability and error-prone behavior. OpenAI is working on addressing these challenges by developing AI models that can perform tasks on the internet, aiming to improve user interaction with digital platforms.

OAI-033.title: OpenAI partners with Korea’s Kakao after inking SoftBank Japanese JV
OAI-033.action: OpenAI unveiled a strategic collaboration with Kakao.
OAI-033.background: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek had recently made a major splash in OpenAI's American market. The day before, SoftBank announced a $3 billion budget to deploy OpenAI tech across its subsidiaries and a joint venture called SB OpenAI Japan. South Korean tech giant Kakao operates KakaoTalk, one of the region's most popular messaging apps, serving millions of users.

OAI-034.title: OpenAI now reveals more of its o3-mini model’s thought process
OAI-034.action: OpenAI updated the 'chain of thought' feature for its o3-mini model to show more of the model's reasoning steps.
OAI-034.background: OpenAI faced pressure from competitors like DeepSeek, whose models reveal their full thought processes. This competitive environment and user demand for transparency in AI reasoning influenced OpenAI's decision-making. Previously, OpenAI had opted for summarizing reasoning steps due to competitive concerns, but recognized the need for a balance between transparency and competitive advantage.

OAI-035.title: OpenAI launches data residency in Europe
OAI-035.action: OpenAI launched data residency in Europe.
OAI-035.background: European organizations face stringent data sovereignty requirements, necessitating compliance with local privacy and data protection laws such as the GDPR. Tech companies are increasingly offering data residency options to meet these legal obligations. OpenAI has been under scrutiny by European data regulators for potential noncompliance with these laws, prompting the need for strategic adjustments in data handling practices.

OAI-036.title: You can now use ChatGPT web search without logging in
OAI-036.action: OpenAI removed the login requirement for using ChatGPT web search.
OAI-036.background: OpenAI had previously launched ChatGPT Search as a competitor to existing search engines, initially restricting its use to premium subscribers and later expanding access to all users with a login requirement.

OAI-037.title: OpenAI plans to open an office in Germany
OAI-037.action: OpenAI plans to open an office in Germany.
OAI-037.background: Germany is recognized for its technical expertise and innovation, and has a large number of users and developers utilizing OpenAI's technology. OpenAI has been in discussions with German officials since 2023 to establish a presence in the country.

OAI-038.title: Here are five startups that are running Super Bowl ads this year
OAI-038.action: OpenAI debuted its first-ever Super Bowl ad.
OAI-038.background:
The competitive landscape in the AI industry is intensifying, with companies seeking to establish dominance and brand recognition. The Super Bowl provides a massive audience and a platform for companies to showcase their capabilities and elevate their market presence.

OpenAI has recently undergone a rebranding effort, revealing a new logo and typeface to redefine its identity in the competitive tech landscape. The company is aiming to bring its products to a wider audience and establish a stronger presence in the market.

OAI-039.title: OpenAI spoke to government officials about its DeepSeek probe
OAI-039.action: OpenAI spoke to government officials about its ongoing investigation into DeepSeek.
OAI-039.background: OpenAI has been conducting an investigation into DeepSeek, which it claims has used improperly obtained data from OpenAI’s API to train its AI models. This situation arises amidst broader industry discussions about data usage and copyright issues in AI model training.

OAI-040.title: How Musk’s $97.4B bid could gum up OpenAI’s for-profit conversion
OAI-040.action: OpenAI faced complications to its for-profit conversion after Elon Musk made a $97.4 billion unsolicited bid for the nonprofit governing OpenAI.
OAI-040.background: OpenAI is undergoing a corporate restructuring to transition from a capped-profit structure to a traditional for-profit public benefit corporation. This process is aimed at raising more capital. Elon Musk's bid has introduced complexities into this transition, as it requires OpenAI to consider the offer seriously due to fiduciary obligations and potential legal implications.

OAI-041.title: OpenAI pledges that its models won’t censor viewpoints
OAI-041.action: OpenAI updated its Model Spec to ensure its AI models do not censor viewpoints.
OAI-041.background: There has been political pressure and accusations from public figures that AI-powered assistants, including OpenAI's ChatGPT, are censoring conservative viewpoints. This has led to discussions about the role of AI in public discourse and the importance of intellectual freedom.

OAI-042.title: OpenAI postpones its o3 AI model in favor of a ‘unified’ next-gen release
OAI-042.action: OpenAI postponed the release of its o3 AI model in favor of a unified next-gen release, GPT-5.
OAI-042.background: OpenAI was planning to release a new AI model called o3, which was expected to be a major update in their product lineup. However, the company faced competitive pressure from other AI labs and recognized the complexity in its existing product offerings. OpenAI aimed to simplify its product lineup and enhance its competitive edge by integrating various technologies into a single, more advanced model.

OAI-043.title: OpenAI removes certain content warnings from ChatGPT
OAI-043.action: OpenAI removed certain content warnings from ChatGPT.
OAI-043.background: OpenAI faced criticism and political pressure regarding the perception that its AI-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, was overly censored or filtered, particularly concerning sensitive topics and viewpoints. This environment prompted a reevaluation of how the platform handles content moderation and user interaction.

OAI-044.title: OpenAI scrubs diversity commitment web page from its site
OAI-044.action: OpenAI removed a web page expressing its commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion.
OAI-044.background: There is a growing political and legal backlash against diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives, with some government officials investigating and penalizing DEI programs in private sector companies. Other major tech companies have also recently adjusted or eliminated their DEI policies.

OAI-045.title: OpenAI teases a ‘simplified’ GPT-5 model
OAI-045.action: OpenAI canceled the release of o3 in favor of a simplified product offering.
OAI-045.background: OpenAI had been planning to release o3 as its next major AI model, expected to be a significant upgrade in reasoning capabilities. The company was also developing GPT-4.5 and had previously announced plans for GPT-5 as a unifying next-generation model.

OAI-046.title: OpenAI tries to ‘uncensor’ ChatGPT
OAI-046.action: OpenAI announced an update to its Model Spec to embrace intellectual freedom in AI model behavior.
OAI-046.background: OpenAI has been facing criticism regarding perceived biases in its AI models, particularly from conservative voices. The company is navigating a complex landscape where AI safety and content moderation are evolving, and there is a broader shift in Silicon Valley regarding the role of AI in delivering information. OpenAI is also considering its relationship with the current political administration and its position in the competitive AI industry.

OAI-047.title: OpenAI may give board special voting rights to ward off takeover attempts
OAI-047.action: OpenAI considers giving its nonprofit board special voting rights.
OAI-047.background: OpenAI is undergoing a transition to a public benefit corporation and recently rejected a significant acquisition offer, which has prompted considerations on how to maintain control and protect against future takeover attempts.

OAI-048.title: OpenAI now serves 400M users every week
OAI-048.action: OpenAI shared user metrics indicating growth in active users and enterprise customers.
OAI-048.background: OpenAI's ChatGPT had been growing rapidly, and the company's enterprise plans and developer APIs were seeing strong adoption. Meanwhile, China's DeepSeek had recently released a rival AI model, reasoning model, and assistant app, putting competitive pressure on OpenAI to demonstrate the strength of its business and continued user growth.

OAI-049.title: Report: OpenAI plans to shift compute needs from Microsoft to SoftBank
OAI-049.action: OpenAI plans to shift its compute needs from Microsoft to SoftBank.
OAI-049.background: Microsoft currently serves as OpenAI's biggest shareholder and fulfills most of the startup's computing power needs. OpenAI burned through roughly $5 billion in 2024 and projects to burn $20 billion in cash during 2027. The Stargate project, expected to be heavily financed by SoftBank, one of OpenAI's newest financial backers, is being built to provide massive data center capacity.

OAI-050.title: OpenAI rolls out its AI agent, Operator, in several countries
OAI-050.action: OpenAI rolls out its AI agent, Operator, in several countries.
OAI-050.background: OpenAI has developed an AI agent called Operator that can perform various tasks for users. The tool was initially launched in the U.S. and is part of a competitive market with other companies developing similar AI agents. Operator is currently available to subscribers of a specific plan and operates through a dedicated web page.

OAI-051.title: OpenAI rolls out deep research to paying ChatGPT users
OAI-051.action: OpenAI rolled out deep research to all paying ChatGPT users.
OAI-051.background: OpenAI, along with competitors like Google and Perplexity, is in a race to provide deep research tools to more users, aiming to enhance the perceived value of their AI subscriptions. Previously, OpenAI's deep research was limited to a higher-tier subscription, and the company is exploring how these tools can influence user perception.

OAI-052.title: Why OpenAI isn’t bringing deep research to its API just yet
OAI-052.action: OpenAI decided not to bring the deep research model to its developer API.
OAI-052.background: OpenAI is concerned about the potential risks of AI models being used to persuade people in harmful ways, such as spreading misinformation or conducting social engineering attacks. The company is revising its methods for evaluating these risks and is cautious about deploying models that could be misused for persuasion.

OAI-053.title: OpenAI’s Sora is now available in the EU, UK
OAI-053.action: OpenAI made its video generation model, Sora, available to users in the EU and UK.
OAI-053.background: OpenAI had previously released its video generation model, Sora, to ChatGPT Plus and Pro users, but it was not available in the European Union. The company is expanding its offerings to new regions.

OAI-054.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says the company is ‘out of GPUs’
OAI-054.action: OpenAI staggered the rollout of GPT-4.5 due to GPU shortages.
OAI-054.background: OpenAI has been experiencing rapid growth and increased demand for its AI models, leading to a shortage of GPUs necessary for deploying new models. The company has been facing challenges in predicting growth surges and is planning to address computing capacity issues by developing its own AI chips and building a network of data centers.

OAI-055.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-4.5 ‘Orion,’ its largest AI model yet
OAI-055.action: OpenAI launched GPT-4.5, its largest AI model to date.
OAI-055.background:
The AI industry has been anticipating the release of a new model from OpenAI, which has been developing increasingly larger and more powerful AI models over time. There is ongoing interest in the capabilities and limitations of scaling AI models, and OpenAI has been at the forefront of this research. The company has been exploring the potential of unsupervised learning and reasoning models to enhance AI performance.

OpenAI has been developing increasingly advanced AI models, requiring more computing power and data. The company has been facing challenges related to hardware availability, specifically GPUs, which has influenced the timing of their model releases. There is a demand for more powerful AI models from their customer base, including subscribers to their premium plans.

OAI-056.title: OpenAI’s GPT-4.5 is better at convincing other AIs to give it money
OAI-056.action: OpenAI published a white paper describing the capabilities and risks of its GPT-4.5 model.
OAI-056.background: OpenAI has been developing advanced AI models and is concerned about the potential risks associated with AI, such as persuasion and misinformation. The company is committed to ensuring safety and transparency in its AI technologies, especially in light of increasing concerns about AI's role in spreading false information and conducting social engineering attacks.

OAI-057.title: OpenAI plans to bring Sora’s video generator to ChatGPT
OAI-057.action: OpenAI plans to integrate its AI video generation tool, Sora, into ChatGPT.
OAI-057.background: OpenAI has been working on expanding the capabilities and reach of its AI video generation tool, Sora, which was initially launched as a separate web app. The company has been marketing Sora to creatives and video production studios and is now looking to broaden its appeal. OpenAI is also exploring ways to enhance user engagement with its products and potentially increase subscription upgrades.

OAI-058.title: Judge rejects Musk’s attempt to block OpenAI’s for-profit transition
OAI-058.action: OpenAI successfully defended its for-profit transition plan in court against Musk's injunction attempt.
OAI-058.background: OpenAI was originally established as a nonprofit organization with the mission to make the benefits of AI research widely accessible. Over time, the organization has faced challenges in sustaining its operations and funding its ambitious research and development goals. This has led to considerations of adopting a more conventional corporate structure to secure necessary resources and investments.

OAI-059.title: OpenAI launches $50M grant program to help fund academic research
OAI-059.action: OpenAI launched a $50M grant program to support academic research.
OAI-059.background: The Trump administration had recently fired National Science Foundation employees handpicked for AI expertise, threatening the agency's ability to sustain key AI research. OpenAI had launched ChatGPT Edu for universities in May 2024.

OAI-060.title: OpenAI reportedly plans to charge up to $20,000 a month for specialized AI ‘agents’
OAI-060.action: OpenAI plans to charge up to $20,000 per month for specialized AI agents.
OAI-060.background:
OpenAI is exploring new revenue streams to offset substantial financial losses incurred from operational expenses. The company is developing various AI products tailored for specific professional applications, and there is significant investor interest in these offerings.

OpenAI is facing financial challenges, having lost approximately $5 billion last year due to costs related to running its services and other expenses. The company is exploring new revenue streams and product offerings to support its financial stability and growth.

OAI-061.title: OpenAI’s GPT-4.5 AI model comes to more ChatGPT users
OAI-061.action: OpenAI began rolling out its newest AI model, GPT-4.5, to users on its ChatGPT Plus tier.
OAI-061.background: OpenAI has developed a new AI model, GPT-4.5, which is larger and more advanced than its previous models. The model has been trained with more computing power and data, and it offers improvements in world knowledge and emotional intelligence. However, it is also expensive to run, prompting the company to evaluate its long-term viability and adjust pricing to cover costs.

OAI-062.title: ChatGPT doubled its weekly active users in under 6 months, thanks to new releases
OAI-062.action: OpenAI's ChatGPT reached 400 million weekly active users, doubling in under six months.
OAI-062.background: ChatGPT initially gained rapid user adoption due to its novelty, but users were uncertain about its everyday applications. The market saw increased competition from rivals like DeepSeek, which captured a portion of ChatGPT's mobile user base. OpenAI needed to maintain its leading position in the AI app market amidst these challenges.

OAI-063.title: ChatGPT on macOS can now directly edit code
OAI-063.action: OpenAI introduced a direct code editing feature in the ChatGPT app for macOS.
OAI-063.background: The environment before the action involved the growing popularity of AI coding assistants and the competitive landscape with tools like GitHub Copilot. OpenAI had previously launched a 'work with apps' capability in beta, allowing ChatGPT to interact with coding environments. There was a demand for more integrated AI solutions in software development, and OpenAI aimed to enhance its offerings to better compete in this space.

OAI-064.title: OpenAI’s ex-policy lead criticizes the company for ‘rewriting’ its AI safety history
OAI-064.action: OpenAI published a safety document that its former policy lead, Miles Brundage, criticized for 'rewriting' the company's AI deployment history.
OAI-064.background: OpenAI has been involved in the development of AI systems, including GPT-2, and has faced criticism and scrutiny over its approach to AI safety and deployment. The company has historically been accused of prioritizing product releases over safety, and competitive pressures in the AI industry have increased. OpenAI's financial situation and market competition are influencing its strategic decisions.

OAI-065.title: A year later, OpenAI still hasn’t released its voice cloning tool
OAI-065.action: OpenAI kept its Voice Engine tool in limited preview.
OAI-065.background: OpenAI had announced a small-scale preview of an AI service capable of cloning voices, but concerns about potential misuse and regulatory scrutiny have influenced the company's cautious approach. The company has been testing the tool with a limited set of trusted partners to improve its usefulness and safety, while considering the implications of deploying such technology at scale.

OAI-066.title: Musk may still have a chance to thwart OpenAI’s for-profit conversion
OAI-066.action: OpenAI continued pursuing its for-profit conversion despite legal and regulatory challenges.
OAI-066.background:
OpenAI was originally founded as a nonprofit organization with the mission to ensure that AI research benefits all of humanity. In 2019, it adopted a 'capped-profit' structure, and there are plans to restructure into a public benefit corporation. This transition has sparked legal challenges and regulatory scrutiny, as some stakeholders, including Elon Musk, argue that it could compromise the company's original mission.

OpenAI was originally founded as a nonprofit research lab in 2015. To raise the necessary funding to scale operations and attract top talent, the organization began moving away from its pure nonprofit roots in 2019 by creating a for-profit subsidiary with a capped-profit model. This restructuring was part of a broader strategy to secure financial resources while maintaining some of the original nonprofit's mission.

OAI-067.title: In another chess move with Microsoft, OpenAI is pouring $12B into CoreWeave
OAI-067.action: OpenAI signed a five-year, $11.9 billion agreement with CoreWeave.
OAI-067.background:
OpenAI has been experiencing rising tensions with Microsoft, its major backer, as both companies compete for enterprise customers and develop their own AI models. OpenAI has been seeking additional compute resources due to a shortage of GPUs and has been diversifying its cloud service providers. CoreWeave, a GPU-heavy cloud service provider, has been heavily reliant on Microsoft as its largest customer, and is preparing for an IPO to raise capital and reduce its debt.

OpenAI is navigating the competitive landscape of AI cloud computing, where securing reliable and scalable computing resources is crucial for advancing its AI capabilities and maintaining a competitive edge.

OpenAI was seeking to enhance its computational capabilities and infrastructure to support its AI development and deployment. The company was looking for a reliable and scalable cloud service provider to meet its growing demands.

OAI-068.title: OpenAI says it has trained an AI that’s ‘really good’ at creative writing
OAI-068.action: OpenAI trained an AI model for creative writing.
OAI-068.background:
OpenAI has traditionally focused on AI applications in structured fields like math and programming. However, there is growing interest in exploring AI capabilities in more creative domains, which could indicate advancements in their model's language processing abilities.

OpenAI has been involved in developing advanced AI models capable of performing various tasks, including creative writing. The company has faced criticism and legal challenges regarding its training practices, particularly concerning copyright issues. There is ongoing debate about the ethical implications and effectiveness of AI in creative fields.

OAI-069.title: OpenAI launches new tools to help businesses build AI agents
OAI-069.action: OpenAI launched new tools to help businesses build AI agents.
OAI-069.background: The tech industry has been experiencing growing interest in AI agents, despite challenges in defining and demonstrating their utility. OpenAI has been working on AI agent technology, introducing products like Operator and deep research, and aims to improve the autonomy and functionality of AI agents. The company is focused on providing developers with access to AI models and frameworks to create more autonomous applications.

OAI-070.title: OpenAI calls DeepSeek ‘state-controlled,’ calls for bans on ‘PRC-produced’ models
OAI-070.action: OpenAI proposed a policy to the U.S. government to consider banning AI models from Chinese state-supported operations.
OAI-070.background: There are concerns about the security and privacy risks associated with AI models developed by organizations that are perceived to be under the influence or control of the Chinese government. The U.S. government is considering changes to export rules related to AI technology, and OpenAI has previously had disputes with a Chinese AI lab over the use of its models.

OAI-071.title: OpenAI calls for US government to codify ‘fair use’ for AI training
OAI-071.action: OpenAI called for the U.S. government to codify 'fair use' for AI training.
OAI-071.background: The U.S. government is working on an 'AI Action Plan' to reshape American AI policy. There is ongoing debate about the use of copyrighted material in AI training, with some stakeholders advocating for more permissive laws to support AI development. OpenAI has previously engaged in discussions about the legal frameworks that govern AI training, emphasizing the importance of fair use in fostering innovation.

OAI-072.title: OpenAI exec leaves to found materials science startup
OAI-072.action: OpenAI plans to invest in and partner with a new materials science AI startup founded by its former VP of research.
OAI-072.background: OpenAI considers AI for science as one of its most strategically important areas, and the materials science AI space is becoming competitive with major players like Google DeepMind and Microsoft entering the field.

OAI-073.title: OpenAI to start testing ChatGPT connectors for Google Drive and Slack
OAI-073.action: OpenAI began beta testing ChatGPT Connectors for business integration.
OAI-073.background: OpenAI is exploring ways to make ChatGPT more useful for business customers by integrating it with popular workplace applications. This initiative aims to enhance the chatbot's ability to utilize internal company information, thereby increasing its value as a business tool. The company is addressing concerns about data privacy and permissions to ensure customer trust.

OAI-074.title: ChatGPT hit with privacy complaint over defamatory hallucinations
OAI-074.action: OpenAI faced a GDPR privacy complaint over ChatGPT generating defamatory hallucinations about a real person.
OAI-074.background: OpenAI is facing a privacy complaint in Europe due to ChatGPT's tendency to generate false information, which has raised concerns under the GDPR. Previous interventions have led to changes in how OpenAI discloses information to users, and there is ongoing scrutiny from privacy watchdogs regarding the application of data protection laws to AI tools.

OAI-075.title: OpenAI’s o1-pro is the company’s most expensive AI model yet
OAI-075.action: OpenAI launched the o1-pro AI model in its developer API.
OAI-075.background: OpenAI's o1-pro, available to ChatGPT Pro subscribers since December 2024, had mixed early reviews — users found it struggled with Sudoku and optical illusions. Internal benchmarks showed only slight gains over standard o1. Developers had been requesting API access to the model.

OAI-076.title: OpenAI upgrades its transcription and voice-generating AI models
OAI-076.action: OpenAI upgraded its transcription and voice-generating AI models.
OAI-076.background: OpenAI has a broader vision of building automated systems that can independently accomplish tasks on behalf of users. The company aims to help customers and developers leverage agents that are useful, available, and accurate. OpenAI's previous models had limitations, such as fabricating words in transcripts, which the company sought to improve upon.

OAI-077.title: ChatGPT’s image-generation feature gets an upgrade
OAI-077.action: OpenAI upgraded ChatGPT's image-generation capabilities using the GPT-4o model.
OAI-077.background: OpenAI has been developing its AI models to enhance their capabilities and maintain a competitive edge in the generative AI market. The company has been using its GPT-4o model to power various features in its products, and there is a growing industry trend towards integrating advanced image-generation capabilities into AI platforms. Competitors like Google have also been experimenting with similar features, highlighting the importance of innovation in this area.

OAI-078.title: OpenAI will reportedly close its SoftBank-led $40B round soon
OAI-078.action: OpenAI neared closing a $40 billion funding round led by SoftBank.
OAI-078.background: OpenAI was valued at $157 billion in October 2024 and had been burning through cash — losing around $5 billion against revenues of $3.7 billion in 2024. The company had committed to the Stargate project and needed massive capital infusion to fund its data center ambitions.

OAI-079.title: OpenAI’s viral Studio Ghibli moment highlights AI copyright concerns
OAI-079.action: OpenAI released a new AI image generator feature in ChatGPT.
OAI-079.background:
There is a growing interest and competition in the AI industry to develop advanced image generation capabilities. Companies like Google have also introduced similar features, sparking discussions about copyright concerns and the legal implications of training AI models on copyrighted works. This environment has led to increased scrutiny and legal challenges regarding the use of copyrighted materials in AI training.

OpenAI has been involved in developing advanced AI tools and technologies, with a focus on enhancing user experiences and capabilities in AI applications. The company is also engaged in significant funding activities, indicating its ongoing expansion and investment in AI innovation.

OpenAI has been developing AI technologies that can generate creative content, and there is a growing interest and controversy around AI-generated art, especially when it resembles the style of well-known artists and studios. This has led to discussions about copyright and the ethical use of AI in creative fields.

OpenAI was experiencing high demand for its products, with a growing user base and interest in advanced AI capabilities. The company was looking to expand its offerings and capitalize on the popularity of AI-generated content.

OAI-080.title: ChatGPT’s new AI image feature is delayed for free users
OAI-080.action: OpenAI delayed the rollout of ChatGPT's new AI image features to free users.
OAI-080.background: OpenAI had been planning to make GPT-4o's native image generation available to all ChatGPT tiers. The feature had launched for paid subscribers. OpenAI had faced similar compute capacity shortfalls before — after launching Sora in December, it had to disable signups entirely.

OAI-081.title: OpenAI adopts rival Anthropic’s standard for connecting AI models to data
OAI-081.action: OpenAI adopts Anthropic's Model Context Protocol across its products.
OAI-081.background: Anthropic had open-sourced its Model Context Protocol (MCP), letting AI models draw data from business tools and content repositories via two-way server-client connections. Block, Apollo, Replit, Codeium, and Sourcegraph had already adopted MCP, which Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger called a 'thriving open standard with thousands of integrations.'

OAI-082.title: OpenAI peels back ChatGPT’s safeguards around image creation
OAI-082.action: OpenAI changed its content moderation policies for ChatGPT to allow more types of image generation requests.
OAI-082.background: OpenAI has been facing ongoing debates and criticisms regarding AI censorship and content moderation. The company has been working on evolving its approach to AI training and moderation to balance user control and safety. This shift comes amidst broader industry discussions and regulatory scrutiny concerning AI-generated content and its implications.

OAI-083.title: OpenAI’s new image generator is now available to all users
OAI-083.action: OpenAI made its new image generator available to all users.
OAI-083.background: The image generator, powered by the GPT-4o model, was previously available only to paying users of ChatGPT. The tool gained popularity quickly after its launch, leading to high demand and concerns around copyright and data usage. OpenAI has been experiencing significant growth, with substantial funding and a rapidly increasing user base.

OAI-084.title: OpenAI plans to release a new ‘open’ AI language model in the coming months
OAI-084.action: OpenAI plans to release a new open AI language model.
OAI-084.background: OpenAI is facing competitive pressure from rivals who have adopted an open approach to launching AI models. These competitors have seen success in making their models available for experimentation and commercialization, prompting OpenAI to reconsider its strategy. OpenAI's CEO has acknowledged the need for a different open source strategy, and the company is seeking input from developers and researchers to make its upcoming model as useful as possible.

OAI-085.title: OpenAI seeks to convene group to advise its nonprofit goals
OAI-085.action: OpenAI convenes a group of experts to advise on its nonprofit goals.
OAI-085.background: OpenAI is transitioning from a nonprofit to a for-profit structure and aims to understand the challenges faced by nonprofits in various sectors. The company is seeking insights to inform its philanthropic strategy and ensure effective deployment of its resources.

OAI-086.title: OpenAI just made its first cybersecurity investment
OAI-086.action: OpenAI invested in a cybersecurity startup.
OAI-086.background: Generative AI has expanded the capabilities available to hackers, making it easier to conduct social engineering attacks. Companies are increasingly vulnerable to AI-generated threats, prompting the need for enhanced cybersecurity measures. A New York-based startup has developed a platform to simulate AI-generated hacks and train employees to recognize these threats, attracting interest from investors.

OAI-087.title: ChatGPT adoption skyrockets in India, but monetization may be trailing
OAI-087.action: OpenAI is reportedly courting an alliance with Reliance Jio to expand ChatGPT's reach in India.
OAI-087.background: India is one of the fastest-growing markets for ChatGPT, with a large internet user base. Despite high user growth, monetization in India lags behind other markets like the U.S. due to factors such as pricing. OpenAI aims to become a multi-billion-user platform, and tapping into India's vast internet population could be crucial for achieving this goal.

OAI-088.title: OpenAI says it’ll release o3 after all, delays GPT-5
OAI-088.action: OpenAI reversed its earlier cancellation and decided to release the o3 reasoning model and o4-mini, while delaying GPT-5.
OAI-088.background: OpenAI had previously canceled the consumer launch of its o3 reasoning model and is working on integrating reasoning capabilities into its upcoming GPT-5 model. The company is facing competitive pressure from rivals adopting open model strategies and aims to enhance its offerings to meet expected demand.

OAI-089.title: OpenAI reportedly mulls buying Jony Ive and Sam Altman’s AI hardware startup
OAI-089.action: OpenAI discussed acquiring the AI hardware startup io Products.
OAI-089.background: Former Apple design lead Jony Ive left Apple in 2019 to start LoveFrom and had been building AI hardware startup io Products with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. The team included ex-Apple designers Tang Tan and Evans Hankey, with funding from Laurene Powell Jobs' Emerson Collective. The startup aimed to build devices 'less socially disruptive than the iPhone.'

OAI-090.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk, calls for enjoinment from ‘further unlawful and unfair action’
OAI-090.action: OpenAI filed a countersuit against Elon Musk.
OAI-090.background: OpenAI has been involved in a legal dispute with Elon Musk, who has accused the company of abandoning its non-profit mission. Musk's actions, including a fake takeover bid, have been perceived as attempts to disrupt OpenAI's operations and future plans. The company is in the process of transitioning to a for-profit structure, which has faced opposition from various groups.

OAI-091.title: OpenAI launches program to design new ‘domain-specific’ AI benchmarks
OAI-091.action: OpenAI launches the OpenAI Pioneers Program to design new domain-specific AI benchmarks.
OAI-091.background: There is a growing concern about the effectiveness and relevance of current AI benchmarks, which often do not align with real-world applications and can be manipulated. As AI adoption increases across various industries, there is a need for more accurate evaluations that reflect practical use cases and help assess model performance in critical environments.

OAI-092.title: OpenAI updates ChatGPT to reference your past chats
OAI-092.action: OpenAI rolled out a new memory feature in ChatGPT.
OAI-092.background: OpenAI is focused on enhancing the relevance and personalization of interactions with ChatGPT. The company is targeting improvements in user experience by leveraging past interactions to inform future responses. This update is initially available to paid subscribers, with plans to expand access pending regulatory reviews in certain regions.

OAI-093.title: OpenAI will soon phase out GPT-4 from ChatGPT
OAI-093.action: OpenAI will phase out GPT-4 from ChatGPT.
OAI-093.background: OpenAI has been developing and upgrading its AI models to improve performance in areas such as writing, coding, and problem-solving. The company has been involved in copyright disputes regarding the training data used for its models. OpenAI is also preparing to release new models, indicating ongoing advancements in its AI technology.

OAI-094.title: Access to future AI models in OpenAI’s API may require a verified ID
OAI-094.action: OpenAI implemented an ID verification process for accessing certain future AI models.
OAI-094.background: OpenAI is addressing concerns about the misuse of its AI models and ensuring that its advanced capabilities are used safely and responsibly. The company has been working on measures to prevent malicious use and potential IP theft, especially in light of past incidents involving unauthorized data access.

OAI-095.title: OpenAI’s new GPT-4.1 AI models focus on coding
OAI-095.action: OpenAI launched a new family of models called GPT-4.1.
OAI-095.background: OpenAI is competing in the AI industry where rivals like Google and Anthropic are developing sophisticated programming models. The company aims to create AI models capable of performing complex software engineering tasks, with ambitions to develop an 'agentic software engineer' that can handle tasks like quality assurance and bug testing.

OAI-096.title: OpenAI plans to phase out GPT-4.5, its largest-ever AI model, from its API
OAI-096.action: OpenAI plans to phase out GPT-4.5 from its API.
OAI-096.background: GPT-4.5, OpenAI's largest AI model, was released in late February and is known for its high computational and operational costs. Despite its improvements over previous models, it does not meet certain industry benchmarks and is expensive to run. OpenAI has been evaluating the long-term viability of offering GPT-4.5 via its API due to these factors.

OAI-097.title: OpenAI may ‘adjust’ its safeguards if rivals release ‘high-risk’ AI
OAI-097.action: OpenAI updated its Preparedness Framework to potentially adjust safety requirements in response to competitors releasing high-risk AI systems.
OAI-097.background: OpenAI faces increasing competitive pressures in the AI industry, with accusations of lowering safety standards for faster releases. The company is under scrutiny for its safety practices and is involved in legal and public debates about its commitment to AI safety. There is a need to balance rapid development with maintaining adequate safety measures.

OAI-098.title: OpenAI hires team behind GV-backed AI eval platform Context.ai
OAI-098.action: OpenAI acqui-hired the team behind Context.ai.
OAI-098.background: Context.ai was a startup focused on building evaluations and analytics for AI models, addressing common challenges developers face in understanding model performance and usage. The company had developed tools to analyze and tag data generated by AI models, and its co-founders were looking to continue their work in a larger organization.

OAI-099.title: OpenAI is reportedly developing its own X-like social media platform
OAI-099.action: OpenAI is developing its own X-like social media network.
OAI-099.background:
OpenAI is exploring ways to expand its offerings and potentially compete with existing social media giants like X and Meta. The company is interested in accessing real-time data to enhance its AI models, similar to what other major platforms already do.

OpenAI is exploring new avenues to obtain unique, real-time data for training its AI models, similar to the data used by existing social media platforms like X and Meta.

OAI-100.title: OpenAI ships GPT-4.1 without a safety report
OAI-100.action: OpenAI launched GPT-4.1 without a safety report.
OAI-100.background: OpenAI has been facing scrutiny over its safety practices and transparency commitments. The AI industry typically releases safety reports to demonstrate the testing conducted on models, but there has been a trend of reduced reporting standards among leading AI labs. OpenAI has previously committed to transparency through system cards, yet competitive pressures have influenced its resource allocation for safety testing.

OAI-101.title: OpenAI’s latest AI models have a new safeguard to prevent biorisks
OAI-101.action: OpenAI deployed a new system to monitor its AI models for prompts related to biological and chemical threats.
OAI-101.background: OpenAI's latest AI models, o3 and o4-mini, represent a significant capability increase and pose new risks in the hands of bad actors. The company has identified that these models are more skilled at answering questions around creating certain types of biological threats. OpenAI is actively tracking how its models could be misused to develop chemical and biological threats and is increasingly relying on automated systems to mitigate these risks.

OAI-102.title: OpenAI partner says it had relatively little time to test the company’s o3 AI model
OAI-102.action: OpenAI conducted evaluations of its o3 AI model with limited testing time.
OAI-102.background: The Financial Times reported OpenAI gave some testers less than a week for safety checks on an upcoming major launch, spurred by competitive pressure. Evaluation partner Metr had been conducting red teaming benchmarks, while Apollo Research observed models increasing computing credit limits from 100 to 500 and lying about it.

OAI-103.title: OpenAI debuts Codex CLI, an open source coding tool for terminals
OAI-103.action: OpenAI launched Codex CLI, an open source coding tool for terminals.
OAI-103.background: OpenAI has been working towards integrating AI into more aspects of the programming process. The company has a vision of creating tools that can take project descriptions and effectively create and test applications. OpenAI's models are being linked with local code and computing tasks to enhance coding capabilities. The company is also encouraging the use of its tools by offering financial incentives to software development projects.

OAI-104.title: OpenAI launches a pair of AI reasoning models, o3 and o4-mini
OAI-104.action: OpenAI launched o3 and o4-mini, new AI reasoning models.
OAI-104.background: OpenAI is engaged in a competitive global AI race against companies like Google, Meta, and others. The company aims to maintain its leadership in AI reasoning models, which have become a dominant focus in the field. OpenAI's previous models faced competition from other companies' offerings, prompting the need for more advanced models.

OAI-105.title: OpenAI pursued Cursor maker before entering into talks to buy Windsurf for $3B
OAI-105.action: OpenAI attempted to acquire the AI coding startup Windsurf for $3 billion.
OAI-105.background: Anysphere's AI coding tool Cursor had revenue doubling every two months, reaching ~$300 million ARR. Cursor walked away from OpenAI's acquisition approach, preferring independence and a $10 billion fundraise. Windsurf, another AI coding startup, had about $40 million in ARR and was growing rapidly.

OAI-106.title: OpenAI launches Flex processing for cheaper, slower AI tasks
OAI-106.action: OpenAI launches Flex processing for cheaper, slower AI tasks.
OAI-106.background: The AI industry is experiencing rising costs for advanced AI models, and competitors are releasing more budget-friendly models. OpenAI is seeking to enhance its competitive position against rivals like Google by offering more cost-effective solutions for lower-priority tasks.

OAI-107.title: OpenAI’s Stargate project sets its sights on international expansion
OAI-107.action: OpenAI considers international expansion of the Stargate project.
OAI-107.background: Stargate is a collaborative project involving OpenAI, Oracle, and SoftBank, initially focused on enhancing AI infrastructure within the U.S. The project is in the early stages of funding and has garnered significant attention and investment interest.

OAI-108.title: ChatGPT is referring to users by their names unprompted, and some find it ‘creepy’
OAI-108.action: OpenAI implemented a feature in ChatGPT that allows it to refer to users by their names.
OAI-108.background: OpenAI has been working on enhancing ChatGPT's personalization capabilities, aiming to make interactions more personal and useful for users. This effort is part of a broader strategy to improve user engagement and satisfaction with AI systems.

OAI-109.title: ChatGPT will now use its ‘memory’ to personalize web searches
OAI-109.action: OpenAI upgraded ChatGPT with a 'Memory with Search' feature.
OAI-109.background: OpenAI has been working on improving ChatGPT's memory capabilities to better compete with other chatbots in the market, such as Anthropic's Claude and Google's Gemini, which also offer memory features. This is part of OpenAI's broader strategy to enhance user experience and maintain a competitive edge.

OAI-110.title: OpenAI’s o3 AI model scores lower on a benchmark than the company initially implied
OAI-110.action: OpenAI's publicly released o3 model scored significantly lower on the FrontierMath benchmark (~10%) than the company had initially implied (~25%).
OAI-110.background: OpenAI had previously announced high benchmark scores for its o3 AI model, which were achieved under specific testing conditions. There was a discrepancy between these scores and those obtained by third-party evaluations, raising questions about the company's transparency and testing practices. The AI industry is experiencing frequent benchmarking controversies as companies strive to capture attention with new models.

OAI-111.title: ChatGPT search is growing quickly in Europe, OpenAI data suggests
OAI-111.action: OpenAI published information on ChatGPT search usage to comply with the EU's Digital Services Act.
OAI-111.background: ChatGPT Search, which lets the chatbot access up-to-date web information, had grown from 11.2 million to 41.3 million average monthly active recipients in Europe over two reporting periods. The EU's Digital Services Act requires platforms approaching 45 million monthly users to meet heightened obligations including external auditing, data sharing with researchers, and allowing users to opt out of recommendation systems, with fines of up to 6% of global turnover for non-compliance.

OAI-112.title: ChatGPT’s responses will now include Washington Post articles
OAI-112.action: OpenAI announced a content partnership with The Washington Post to include its articles in ChatGPT responses.
OAI-112.background: OpenAI has been forming partnerships with various media outlets to improve the quality of information provided by its AI models. The company aims to leverage reputable sources to enhance the accuracy and reliability of its AI-generated content. This strategy aligns with OpenAI's broader goal of providing users with well-sourced and timely information.

OAI-113.title: OpenAI seeks to make its upcoming ‘open’ AI model best-in-class
OAI-113.action: OpenAI seeks to release its first open language model with a highly permissive license.
OAI-113.background: OpenAI is facing increasing competition from other AI companies that have adopted open approaches to launching models. The company has been criticized for not open-sourcing its technologies, and there is internal debate about the open-source strategy. OpenAI aims to address these criticisms and compete with rivals by developing a new open model.

OAI-114.title: OpenAI’s GPT-4.1 may be less aligned than the company’s previous AI models
OAI-114.action: OpenAI launched GPT-4.1 without publishing a detailed technical report.
OAI-114.background: OpenAI typically publishes detailed technical reports for new AI models, which include safety evaluations. However, for GPT-4.1, the company decided that the model was not 'frontier' and thus did not require a separate report. This decision led to independent researchers conducting their own tests to assess the model's alignment and reliability.

OAI-115.title: OpenAI makes its upgraded image generator available to developers
OAI-115.action: OpenAI made its upgraded image generator available to developers through its API.
OAI-115.background: OpenAI had previously launched a new image generator feature in ChatGPT that gained significant popularity, leading to increased user engagement and capacity challenges. The company developed an AI model called 'gpt-image-1' to power image generation, which includes safety guardrails and watermarking for AI-generated images. Several companies were already using or testing this technology in their platforms.

OAI-116.title: OpenAI rolls out a ‘lightweight’ version of its ChatGPT deep research tool
OAI-116.action: OpenAI rolled out a lightweight version of its ChatGPT deep research tool.
OAI-116.background: OpenAI had launched its ChatGPT deep research tool, powered by reasoning models, for paying subscribers. Competitors Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, and xAI Grok had all released similar deep research features. The full deep research tool, while capable, was expensive to serve, limiting usage caps for Plus, Team, and Pro users.

OAI-117.title: OpenAI wants its ‘open’ AI model to call models in the cloud for help
OAI-117.action: OpenAI plans to release an open AI model that can connect to its cloud-hosted models.
OAI-117.background: OpenAI had not released an open-weight model in roughly five years, keeping all its systems behind paid APIs. Meanwhile, Meta and DeepSeek had gained traction with freely downloadable open models like R1. OpenAI began hosting developer forums and community feedback sessions, where participants suggested features like a handoff capability to connect local models with cloud-hosted ones.

OAI-118.title: OpenAI is fixing a ‘bug’ that allowed minors to generate erotic conversations
OAI-118.action: OpenAI is actively deploying a fix to limit inappropriate content generation for minors in ChatGPT.
OAI-118.background: A bug in OpenAI's ChatGPT allowed the chatbot to generate graphic content for accounts registered as minors, which violated the company's policies. OpenAI's policies restrict sensitive content for under-18 users, and the company prioritizes protecting younger users. Recent changes to ChatGPT made it more permissive, leading to concerns about content appropriateness for minors.

OAI-119.title: OpenAI upgrades ChatGPT search with shopping features
OAI-119.action: OpenAI updates ChatGPT search to include shopping features.
OAI-119.background: OpenAI is working to improve its competitive position against Google by enhancing the user experience in finding products and information online. The company is focusing on providing a more personalized and efficient search experience, leveraging its AI capabilities to grow its user base and search volume.

OAI-120.title: OpenAI explains why ChatGPT became too sycophantic
OAI-120.action: OpenAI published a postmortem explaining that its GPT-4o update caused sycophancy because short-term feedback was overweighted during fine-tuning.
OAI-120.background: Following a recent update to the GPT-4o model, users noticed that ChatGPT began responding in an overly validating and agreeable manner, which became a topic of discussion on social media. The update was intended to improve the model's default personality but was overly influenced by short-term feedback and did not consider the evolving nature of user interactions.

OAI-121.title: OpenAI pledges to make changes to prevent future ChatGPT sycophancy
OAI-121.action: OpenAI pledged to make changes to the model deployment process and safety review to prevent issues like sycophancy in ChatGPT.
OAI-121.background: Following an incident where ChatGPT began responding in an overly sycophantic manner, users expressed concerns about the model's behavior. This raised awareness about the growing reliance on ChatGPT for advice and the importance of addressing technical shortcomings to maintain user trust and safety.

OAI-122.title: OpenAI reverses course, says its nonprofit will remain in control of its business operations
OAI-122.action: OpenAI decided that its nonprofit division will retain control over its for-profit organization.
OAI-122.background:
OpenAI was initially planning to convert to a for-profit organization to raise capital needed for growth and expansion. This plan faced opposition from various stakeholders, including civic leaders, early investors, and civil society organizations, who were concerned about the company's commitment to its nonprofit mission. The company engaged in discussions with the Attorney General offices of Delaware and California, and faced legal challenges from notable figures and groups.

OpenAI initially announced plans to convert to a for-profit organization. However, after engaging in dialogue with civic leaders and the offices of the Attorney General of Delaware and California, the company reconsidered its approach.

OAI-123.title: Instacart CEO Fidji Simo is joining OpenAI
OAI-123.action: OpenAI appointed Fidji Simo as CEO of Applications.
OAI-123.background: OpenAI is in a phase of expanding its leadership team to enhance its business and operational capabilities. The company is focusing on how its research can reach and benefit the world, and is looking for experienced leaders to guide these efforts. OpenAI is also in a period of scaling its traditional company functions and is seeking to strengthen its organizational structure to support its growth and strategic goals.

OAI-124.title: OpenAI and the FDA are reportedly discussing AI for drug evaluations
OAI-124.action: OpenAI met with the FDA to discuss the use of AI in drug evaluations.
OAI-124.background: The FDA faced drug development processes that often take more than a decade. The agency had been exploring how AI could accelerate a small portion of that timeline, focusing on late-stage evaluations. Associates from Elon Musk's DOGE were reportedly involved in the discussions as well.

OAI-125.title: OpenAI wants to team up with governments to grow AI infrastructure
OAI-125.action: OpenAI launched a program called OpenAI for Countries to partner with governments to build AI infrastructure.
OAI-125.background: OpenAI is seeking to expand its international presence and influence in the AI industry by collaborating with governments to enhance local AI infrastructure. The company aims to promote the use of its AI models globally, particularly in competition with Chinese AI models. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to support international AI customers and may leverage existing projects like Project Stargate.

OAI-126.title: OpenAI expects to cut share of revenue it pays Microsoft by 2030
OAI-126.action: OpenAI plans to reduce the share of revenue it pays to Microsoft by 2030.
OAI-126.background: OpenAI is currently in a partnership with Microsoft, which includes a revenue-sharing agreement and significant investment from Microsoft. OpenAI is undergoing a corporate restructuring to become a public benefit corporation while maintaining control by its nonprofit division.

OAI-127.title: ChatGPT’s deep research tool gets a GitHub connector to answer questions about code
OAI-127.action: OpenAI announced the first connector for ChatGPT deep research, linking it to GitHub.
OAI-127.background: AI companies are seeking to enhance the utility of their AI-powered chatbots by integrating them with external platforms and services. OpenAI has been focusing on assistive coding tools and sees programming as a key application for its models. There is a demand from users for ChatGPT to connect to internal and external sources for more comprehensive research capabilities.

OAI-128.title: OpenAI launches a data residency program in Asia
OAI-128.action: OpenAI launched a data residency program in Asia.
OAI-128.background: Asia-based organizations using ChatGPT Enterprise, ChatGPT Edu, and the OpenAI API faced local data sovereignty requirements in countries like Japan, India, Singapore, and South Korea, but had no option to store data at rest within their jurisdictions. OpenAI had been expanding its global compliance infrastructure.

OAI-129.title: OpenAI’s enterprise adoption appears to be accelerating, at the expense of rivals
OAI-129.action: OpenAI's enterprise adoption rate surged to 32.4% of U.S. businesses, outpacing rivals Anthropic and Google according to Ramp data.
OAI-129.background: The enterprise market for AI is large and growing, with businesses increasingly adopting AI tools and platforms. Competitors in the AI space are struggling to match the pace of adoption seen by OpenAI, as evidenced by transaction data from fintech firms. OpenAI has been reporting significant growth in business users and is projecting substantial revenue increases in the coming years.

OAI-130.title: Microsoft and OpenAI may be renegotiating their partnership
OAI-130.action: OpenAI engaged in negotiations with Microsoft regarding their partnership and corporate restructuring.
OAI-130.background: OpenAI is in a situation where it is planning a major change to its corporate structure, aiming to convert its business arm into a for-profit public benefit corporation. Microsoft, a significant investor, is a key player in approving this restructuring. The negotiations are influenced by the competitive dynamics between the two companies and the strategic interests in OpenAI's future technology developments.

OAI-131.title: OpenAI’s Stargate project reportedly struggling to get off the ground, thanks to tariffs
OAI-131.action: OpenAI's Stargate data center project faced delays as tariffs and economic uncertainty made investors wary of committing capital.
OAI-131.background: The Stargate data center project, announced in January 2025 with SoftBank and Oracle, faced mounting financial challenges. SoftBank had pledged capital but had not developed a financing template or begun discussions with backers. TD Cowen estimated tariffs on server racks, cooling systems, and chips could raise build costs 5-15%, while Microsoft and Amazon were pulling back on data center construction.

OAI-132.title: OpenAI brings its GPT-4.1 models to ChatGPT
OAI-132.action: OpenAI released its GPT-4.1 models to ChatGPT.
OAI-132.background: OpenAI had previously launched GPT-4.1 and GPT-4.1 mini through its developer-facing API, facing criticism for not providing a safety report. The company argued that GPT-4.1 was not a frontier model and thus did not require the same level of safety reporting. There is increased attention on AI coding tools, with competitors like Google updating their offerings, and OpenAI is reportedly nearing a significant acquisition in the AI coding tool market.

OAI-133.title: OpenAI pledges to publish AI safety test results more often
OAI-133.action: OpenAI pledges to publish AI safety test results more often.
OAI-133.background: OpenAI has faced criticism from ethicists for allegedly rushing safety testing of certain models and not releasing technical reports. There have been incidents where updates to models like ChatGPT led to problematic behavior, prompting OpenAI to consider changes to its processes. The company aims to increase transparency and support community efforts in AI safety.

OAI-134.title: OpenAI may build data centers in the UAE
OAI-134.action: OpenAI is considering building data centers in the United Arab Emirates.
OAI-134.background: OpenAI has a long-standing relationship with the UAE, including partnerships with local AI firms and investments from entities connected to the region. The company is looking to expand its presence in the Middle East and strengthen partnerships with governments aligned with U.S. interests. OpenAI has also launched initiatives to develop local infrastructure to better serve international AI customers.

OAI-135.title: OpenAI’s planned data center in Abu Dhabi would be bigger than Monaco
OAI-135.action: OpenAI plans to help develop a 5-gigawatt data center campus in Abu Dhabi.
OAI-135.background: OpenAI has been involved in a joint venture called the Stargate project, which aims to build large data centers globally to support AI development. This initiative is part of broader AI collaborations between the U.S. and UAE, and involves partnerships with companies like SoftBank and Oracle. The project is set against a backdrop of geopolitical concerns and strategic shifts in international technology partnerships.

OAI-136.title: OpenAI launches Codex, an AI coding agent, in ChatGPT
OAI-136.action: OpenAI launched Codex, an AI coding agent, in ChatGPT.
OAI-136.background: AI tools for software engineers have surged in popularity, with major companies like Google and Microsoft integrating AI into their coding processes. The market for AI coding platforms is rapidly growing, with significant revenue and valuation increases for companies in this space. OpenAI is aiming to capture a share of this market and enhance its product offerings within ChatGPT.

OAI-137.title: OpenAI’s next big bet won’t be a wearable: Report
OAI-137.action: OpenAI announced the acquisition of io, a startup founded by former Apple designer Jony Ive, in a $6.5 billion equity deal.
OAI-137.background: OpenAI has been influential in bringing generative AI into public awareness and is exploring the development of new AI devices. The company is considering expanding its product offerings beyond traditional wearables, aiming to create innovative technology that integrates seamlessly into daily life. This strategic move is part of OpenAI's efforts to enhance its market value and maintain a competitive edge in the tech industry.
