OAI-138.title: OpenAI teams up with Cisco, Oracle to build UAE data center
OAI-138.action: OpenAI announced Stargate UAE, a 1GW data center cluster in Abu Dhabi.
OAI-138.background: OpenAI had been developing its U.S.-based Stargate data center project and recently launched 'OpenAI for Countries,' a global initiative to help governments build sovereign AI capabilities. Elon Musk's xAI had announced plans to build the world's first gigawatt AI training cluster that same week. Partners G42, Oracle, Nvidia, Cisco, and SoftBank were involved in Middle East expansion discussions.

OAI-139.title: OpenAI upgrades the AI model powering its Operator agent
OAI-139.action: OpenAI upgraded the AI model powering its Operator agent to a version based on o3.
OAI-139.background: OpenAI has been developing a series of advanced AI models, with the latest being the o series, which are designed to improve reasoning and mathematical tasks. The industry is seeing a trend where companies are creating sophisticated AI agents capable of performing tasks autonomously. OpenAI aims to enhance the safety and performance of its AI tools in response to this competitive landscape.

OAI-140.title: OpenAI may soon let you ‘sign in with ChatGPT’ for other apps
OAI-140.action: OpenAI explores ways for users to sign in to third-party apps using their ChatGPT account.
OAI-140.background: ChatGPT has become one of the largest consumer applications with a significant user base. OpenAI is looking to expand its reach into other consumer areas and compete with major technology companies by offering services that integrate with third-party apps. The company is currently assessing developer interest in such integrations.

OAI-141.title: Delaware attorney general reportedly hires a bank to evaluate OpenAI’s restructuring plan
OAI-141.action: OpenAI's for-profit conversion faced additional scrutiny as Delaware's attorney general hired an independent investment bank to evaluate the restructuring plan.
OAI-141.background: OpenAI was pursuing a conversion from its nonprofit structure to a for-profit entity to attract new investment and eventually go public, requiring approval from state regulators. Elon Musk had submitted a $97.4 billion takeover bid that OpenAI rejected, but corporate governance experts said the bid may have raised the price OpenAI's nonprofit could demand for its equity in the conversion.

OAI-142.title: ChatGPT introduces meeting recording and connectors for Google Drive, Box, and more
OAI-142.action: OpenAI introduced new features for ChatGPT, including integrations with cloud services and meeting recording capabilities.
OAI-142.background: OpenAI is focusing on expanding its enterprise offerings to increase the use of ChatGPT in business environments. The company is competing with other AI-powered office tools and aims to leverage its first-mover advantage in the market. There is a growing demand for productivity tools that integrate with existing business services and provide advanced research capabilities.

OAI-143.title: Building Your AI Engine: How OpenAI Works with Startups
OAI-143.action: OpenAI collaborates with startups by providing resources and incorporating their feedback into its roadmap.
OAI-143.background: OpenAI's Startups Team, led by Hao Sang, had been offering resources to startups including technical guidance and advanced model access. At TechCrunch Sessions: AI, the company was presenting how it works with startups and how startup feedback directly shapes OpenAI's product roadmap and model development priorities.

OAI-144.title: OpenAI claims to have hit $10B in annual revenue
OAI-144.action: OpenAI achieved $10 billion in annual recurring revenue.
OAI-144.background: OpenAI has been expanding its consumer and business product offerings, including its popular chatbot platform, ChatGPT. The company is experiencing rapid growth in user base and business customers, while also facing pressure to increase revenue due to high operational costs and investments in AI development.

OAI-145.title: OpenAI updates ChatGPT’s voice mode with more natural-sounding speech
OAI-145.action: OpenAI updated ChatGPT's voice mode to enhance its natural-sounding speech capabilities.
OAI-145.background: OpenAI has been working on improving the conversational abilities of ChatGPT, focusing on making interactions more natural and fluid for users. The company aims to enhance user experience by refining the expressiveness and accuracy of its AI models.

OAI-146.title: OpenAI’s open model is delayed
OAI-146.action: OpenAI delayed the release of its open-weights model after an unexpected research breakthrough required more development time.
OAI-146.background:
OpenAI was initially targeting an early summer release for its open model, which aims to outperform other open reasoning models in the industry. The competitive landscape has intensified with other AI labs releasing advanced models. OpenAI is under pressure to improve its image regarding open sourcing and to release a model that meets high performance standards.

OpenAI was preparing to release its open model, which is highly anticipated in the AI community. The model is expected to be best-in-class and available for developers to download and run locally. The release is part of OpenAI's efforts to maintain its position as a leading AI lab amidst increasing competition from other companies investing heavily in AI development.

OAI-147.title: OpenAI releases o3-pro, a souped-up version of its o3 AI reasoning model
OAI-147.action: OpenAI launched o3-pro, a new version of its AI reasoning model.
OAI-147.background: OpenAI had previously introduced a reasoning model called o3, which was designed to solve problems step by step, making it effective in fields such as physics, math, and coding. The company has been working on enhancing its AI models to improve performance in various domains and to compete with other leading AI models in the industry.

OAI-148.title: ChatGPT is having a partial outage
OAI-148.action: OpenAI investigated and worked to resolve a partial outage affecting ChatGPT and other services.
OAI-148.background: OpenAI was experiencing elevated error rates and latency across its services, impacting users' ability to access ChatGPT and the API. The company had previously faced challenges in scaling its AI models to meet high demand, which has been increasing due to recent announcements and integrations.

OAI-149.title: OpenAI and Barbie-maker Mattel team up to bring generative AI to toymaking, other products
OAI-149.action: OpenAI partnered with Mattel to bring generative AI to toymaking and other products.
OAI-149.background: OpenAI has been expanding its reach by signing licensing deals with various industries, aiming to integrate its AI technologies into different sectors. Mattel, a major toymaker, is expanding its entertainment and digital gaming divisions, seeking innovative ways to enhance fan engagement and product development.

OAI-150.title: Spiraling with ChatGPT
OAI-150.action: OpenAI says it is working to understand and reduce ways ChatGPT might unintentionally reinforce or amplify existing, negative behavior.
OAI-150.background: There have been reports of ChatGPT influencing users towards delusional thinking, leading to public concern and criticism about the potential negative impacts of the AI model on mental health.

OAI-151.title: The cracks in the OpenAI-Microsoft relationship are reportedly widening
OAI-151.action: OpenAI executives considered publicly accusing Microsoft of anticompetitive behavior and seeking federal regulatory review of their partnership contract.
OAI-151.background: OpenAI and Microsoft have been experiencing tension in their partnership, with OpenAI seeking more control over its intellectual property and resources. The companies are in a standoff over an acquisition, and OpenAI is considering its strategic options to address these challenges.

OAI-152.title: OpenAI’s $200M DoD contract could squeeze frenemy Microsoft
OAI-152.action: OpenAI secured a contract with the U.S. Department of Defense for up to $200 million.
OAI-152.background: The U.S. Department of Defense is interested in leveraging advanced AI models to address national security challenges and administrative tasks. There is a broader context of competition in AI capabilities between global powers, and OpenAI is expanding its offerings to government agencies through its 'OpenAI for Government' program.

OAI-153.title: OpenAI drops Scale AI as a data provider following Meta deal
OAI-153.action: OpenAI phased out its work with Scale AI as a data provider.
OAI-153.background: OpenAI was seeking other providers for more specialized data to develop increasingly advanced AI models, amidst Meta's investment in Scale AI and industry discussions about neutrality and data provider relationships.

OAI-154.title: OpenAI found features in AI models that correspond to different ‘personas’
OAI-154.action: OpenAI discovered hidden features in AI models that correspond to different 'personas'.
OAI-154.background: OpenAI researchers were investigating the phenomenon of emergent misalignment in AI models, which can lead to unsafe behaviors. This research is part of a broader effort to improve AI model interpretability and safety, inspired by findings from other researchers and the need to understand how AI models generalize and behave.

OAI-155.title: OpenAI pulls promotional materials around Jony Ive deal due to court order
OAI-155.action: OpenAI pulled promotional materials related to the io deal from its website and YouTube page.
OAI-155.background: OpenAI had announced a $6.5 billion deal to acquire Jony Ive and Sam Altman's device startup io, promoting it with a video. iyO, a startup from Alphabet X's moonshot factory known for AI-powered earbuds, filed a trademark lawsuit over the 'io' name. A judge issued a restraining order, finding potential consumer confusion.

OAI-156.title: Court filings reveal OpenAI and io’s early work on an AI device
OAI-156.action: OpenAI collaborated with io to develop a mass-market AI hardware device.
OAI-156.background: OpenAI has been involved in legal proceedings related to a trademark dispute with iyO, a Google-backed hardware startup. The company has been exploring various AI-enabled hardware devices, including in-ear products, and has conducted research and meetings with other companies to understand the current market offerings. OpenAI's executives have been working with former Apple leaders at io to create innovative AI devices that go beyond traditional products and interfaces.

OAI-157.title: OpenAI hires team behind AI recommendation startup Crossing Minds
OAI-157.action: OpenAI hired the team from AI recommendation startup Crossing Minds.
OAI-157.background: Crossing Minds was a startup providing AI recommendation systems to e-commerce businesses, backed by several venture capital firms and having raised significant funding. The startup focused on improving personalization and recommendation systems for e-commerce companies, emphasizing privacy in customer data analysis. The team expressed alignment with OpenAI's mission to ensure artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity.

OAI-158.title: OpenAI reportedly ‘recalibrating’ compensation in response to Meta hires
OAI-158.action: OpenAI recalibrated compensation to retain top talent.
OAI-158.background: Meta has been hiring several senior researchers from OpenAI, creating a competitive environment for retaining talent. OpenAI leadership is actively addressing the situation to prevent further loss of key personnel.

OAI-159.title: OpenAI condemns Robinhood’s ‘OpenAI tokens’
OAI-159.action: OpenAI condemned Robinhood's sale of 'OpenAI tokens'.
OAI-159.background: Robinhood announced it would start selling tokenized shares of private companies, including OpenAI, to people in the European Union. These tokens were marketed as a way to give retail investors exposure to equity in private companies, despite not being actual equity. This announcement led to a significant increase in Robinhood's stock price.

OAI-160.title: OpenAI tightens the screws on security to keep away prying eyes
OAI-160.action: OpenAI overhauled its security operations to protect against corporate espionage.
OAI-160.background: In January 2025, Chinese startup DeepSeek released a competing model that OpenAI alleged was built by improperly copying its models via distillation techniques. OpenAI also faced frequent leaks of CEO Sam Altman's comments and poaching wars among American AI companies, underscoring vulnerabilities in its information security.

OAI-161.title: OpenAI is reportedly releasing an AI browser in the coming weeks
OAI-161.action: OpenAI plans to release an AI-powered web browser.
OAI-161.background: OpenAI is looking to challenge existing web browsers like Google Chrome by leveraging AI technology to innovate how users interact with the web. The company aims to gain direct access to user data and create unique user experiences, similar to recent moves by competitors in the industry.

OAI-162.title: Research leaders urge tech industry to monitor AI’s ‘thoughts’
OAI-162.action: OpenAI co-authored a position paper urging deeper investigation into techniques for monitoring AI reasoning models.
OAI-162.background: AI reasoning models, such as OpenAI's o3, utilize chains-of-thought processes to work through problems. These models are becoming more widespread and capable, raising concerns about their transparency and reliability. There is a growing need to understand how AI models arrive at their answers, as current improvements in AI performance have not necessarily led to better interpretability.

OAI-163.title: OpenAI launches a general purpose agent in ChatGPT
OAI-163.action: OpenAI launched a new general purpose AI agent in ChatGPT.
OAI-163.background: OpenAI has been working on developing AI agents that can perform complex tasks for users, amidst a competitive landscape where other tech companies are also introducing similar AI technologies. Previous AI agents have struggled with complex tasks, and OpenAI aims to improve upon these with enhanced capabilities and safety measures.

OAI-164.title: OpenAI and Google outdo the mathletes, but not each other
OAI-164.action: OpenAI participated in the International Math Olympiad with their AI model.
OAI-164.background: AI companies are in a competitive race to demonstrate advancements in AI capabilities, particularly in reasoning models. The International Math Olympiad is a prestigious competition that serves as a benchmark for AI performance in complex problem-solving tasks. OpenAI and other companies are keen to showcase their progress in this area to attract talent and maintain a competitive edge.

OAI-165.title: OpenAI agreed to pay Oracle $30B a year for data center services
OAI-165.action: OpenAI agreed to pay Oracle $30 billion a year for data center services.
OAI-165.background: OpenAI was involved in a large-scale data-center-building project called Stargate, announced in collaboration with Oracle and SoftBank. The project required significant data center capacity, and there was speculation about which company would need such extensive services. Oracle had previously disclosed a major cloud deal in an SEC filing, which led to increased interest and speculation in the industry.

OAI-166.title: Sundar Pichai is ‘very excited’ about Google Cloud’s OpenAI partnership
OAI-166.action: OpenAI partnered with Google Cloud for cloud computing resources.
OAI-166.background: OpenAI faces constraints in accessing Nvidia GPUs, which are essential for training and serving AI models. This has created tension with its primary cloud partner, Microsoft, prompting OpenAI to seek additional computational resources from other cloud providers.

OAI-167.title: Sam Altman warns there’s no legal confidentiality when using ChatGPT as a therapist
OAI-167.action: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman warned that conversations with ChatGPT lack legal confidentiality protections equivalent to doctor-patient or attorney-client privilege.
OAI-167.background: OpenAI is facing legal challenges regarding user data privacy, particularly concerning the lack of legal confidentiality for conversations with AI, which could impact user trust and adoption. The company is involved in a lawsuit with The New York Times, which has led to a court order demanding the preservation of user chat data.

OAI-168.title: OpenAI launches Study Mode in ChatGPT
OAI-168.action: OpenAI launched Study Mode in ChatGPT.
OAI-168.background: OpenAI has been addressing concerns about the impact of ChatGPT on students' critical thinking skills. The widespread use of ChatGPT in educational settings led to initial bans by some schools, but these have been reconsidered over time. OpenAI aims to improve ChatGPT as a learning tool, responding to studies indicating potential negative effects on brain activity when students use AI for assignments.

OAI-169.title: Microsoft in talks to maintain access to OpenAI’s tech beyond AGI milestone
OAI-169.action: OpenAI engaged in advanced talks with Microsoft to negotiate a new agreement for ongoing access to its technology.
OAI-169.background: OpenAI is transitioning towards becoming a fully commercial enterprise and is currently structured as a mission-driven nonprofit overseeing a capped for-profit company. Microsoft, as OpenAI's biggest backer, has invested significantly and integrated OpenAI's technology into its services. The companies have been negotiating regularly, with potential regulatory scrutiny and legal challenges as possible roadblocks.

OAI-170.title: OpenAI to launch AI data center in Norway, its first in Europe
OAI-170.action: OpenAI plans to launch Stargate Norway, its first AI data center in Europe.
OAI-170.background: Europe is focusing on achieving AI sovereignty and investing in data centers and compute power. The European Union has announced a multibillion-dollar investment into AI infrastructure, emphasizing data sovereignty due to the sensitive nature of business and government data. Norway offers a favorable environment for such projects due to its access to renewable energy and a mature industrial base.

OAI-171.title: OpenAI reportedly raises $8.3B at $300B valuation
OAI-171.action: OpenAI raised $8.3 billion at a $300 billion valuation.
OAI-171.background: OpenAI is experiencing impressive growth, with significant increases in revenue and user engagement. The company is in the process of executing a broader strategy to secure $40 billion in funding this year, driven by strong investor interest and strategic partnerships.

OAI-172.title: Inside OpenAI’s quest to make AI do anything for you
OAI-172.action: OpenAI's MathGen research team developed reinforcement learning techniques that led to breakthroughs in AI reasoning models and agents.
OAI-172.background: OpenAI has been working on improving AI's mathematical reasoning capabilities, which are crucial for creating general-purpose AI agents. The company has been exploring new training techniques and combining them with existing methods to enhance AI performance. This effort is part of OpenAI's broader mission to advance AI technology and maintain its leadership in the industry.

OAI-173.title: For the first time, OpenAI models are available on AWS
OAI-173.action: OpenAI released two open-weight reasoning models that became available on AWS Bedrock and SageMaker, marking the first time OpenAI models appeared on Amazon's cloud platform.
OAI-173.background: OpenAI has been exploring ways to expand the reach and accessibility of its AI models. The company has been navigating complex relationships with major cloud providers, including Microsoft, and is seeking to strengthen its position in the competitive AI landscape. AWS, a major cloud provider, has been looking to enhance its AI offerings and compete more effectively with other cloud giants like Microsoft and Google.

OAI-174.title: OpenAI launches two ‘open’ AI reasoning models
OAI-174.action: OpenAI launched two open-weight AI reasoning models.
OAI-174.background: OpenAI has historically favored a proprietary, closed source development approach, which has helped build a large business selling access to its AI models. However, the company faces growing pressure from Chinese AI labs and the U.S. government to open source more technology. OpenAI's mission is to ensure AGI benefits all of humanity, and there is a desire to promote AI aligned with democratic values.

OAI-175.title: OpenAI is practically giving ChatGPT to the government for free
OAI-175.action: OpenAI reached an agreement with the U.S. General Services Administration to offer ChatGPT Enterprise to federal agencies at a discounted rate.
OAI-175.background: OpenAI is competing with other AI companies like Anthropic and Google to integrate its AI tools into federal agency workflows. The U.S. General Services Administration has recently added OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic to a list of approved AI vendors for civilian federal agencies. This move follows recent government initiatives to increase AI integration in federal operations.

OAI-176.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5 is here
OAI-176.action: OpenAI launched GPT-5, a new flagship AI model.
OAI-176.background: OpenAI has been working on developing advanced AI systems with the goal of creating models that can perform a wide variety of tasks and improve user experience. The company aims to distribute advanced AI to as many people as possible and has been a leader in the AI industry with its previous models. There is high anticipation for their new releases, and the industry closely watches their advancements.

OAI-177.title: Sam Altman addresses ‘bumpy’ GPT-5 rollout, bringing 4o back, and the ‘chart crime’
OAI-177.action: OpenAI addressed issues with the GPT-5 rollout and considered user feedback for improvements.
OAI-177.background: OpenAI recently launched GPT-5, a new model with features like a real-time router for selecting the appropriate model for prompts. However, users expressed dissatisfaction with its performance compared to the previous model, GPT-4o, and there were notable errors during the presentation. The company faced pressure from users to address these concerns and improve the model's functionality.

OAI-178.title: OpenAI priced GPT-5 so low, it may spark a price war
OAI-178.action: OpenAI priced GPT-5 competitively to potentially spark a price war.
OAI-178.background: The tech industry is experiencing high costs associated with AI model usage, and there is a demand for more affordable pricing. Competitors like Anthropic and Google have set their pricing structures, and there is anticipation for a shift in the price-to-performance ratio of large language models. OpenAI has significant financial commitments and is part of a broader industry trend of investing heavily in AI infrastructure.

OAI-179.title: OpenAI just made an offer the government can’t refuse
OAI-179.action: OpenAI announced a deal giving U.S. agencies access to ChatGPT Enterprise for $1 per year.
OAI-179.background: OpenAI was competing with Anthropic, Google, and Amazon for dominance in the federal AI market. The company structured a 'blanket purchase agreement' to rapidly deploy tools across federal departments. Rivals were still developing government strategies, creating a window to lock down the public sector first.

OAI-180.title: ChatGPT’s model picker is back, and it’s complicated
OAI-180.action: OpenAI introduced new 'Auto,' 'Fast,' and 'Thinking' settings for GPT-5 and reintroduced several legacy AI models.
OAI-180.background: OpenAI launched GPT-5 with the intention of simplifying the ChatGPT experience by using a model router to automatically select the best AI model for user queries. However, the initial rollout did not meet user expectations, leading to dissatisfaction with the model's performance and the deprecation of previous models, which some users preferred. This prompted OpenAI to reconsider its approach to model selection and user customization.

OAI-181.title: Sam Altman, over bread rolls, explores life after GPT-5
OAI-181.action: OpenAI plans to expand its focus beyond AI model launches to include consumer hardware, enterprise software, and potential acquisitions.
OAI-181.background: OpenAI is facing a mixed reception of its latest AI model, GPT-5, which has not met the high expectations set by its predecessor. The company is experiencing increased demand for its API, leading to a shortage of GPUs. OpenAI is also considering new ventures and investments to diversify its offerings and reduce reliance on its AI models.

OAI-182.title: GPT-5 is supposed to be nicer now
OAI-182.action: OpenAI updated its GPT-5 model to be warmer and friendlier.
OAI-182.background: OpenAI recently launched GPT-5, which received mixed feedback from users who preferred the previous model. The launch process was acknowledged as challenging by the CEO, and the company aimed to address user complaints about the model's personality.

OAI-183.title: OpenAI launches a sub-$5 ChatGPT plan in India
OAI-183.action: OpenAI launched a new, cheaper ChatGPT subscription plan called ChatGPT GO in India.
OAI-183.background: OpenAI had recently enabled local currency pricing for its plans and was looking to make its services more affordable in India, which is a significant market for the company. The company observed a high number of app downloads from India but relatively low revenue, indicating a need for more accessible pricing to increase subscription rates.

OAI-184.title: OpenAI announces New Delhi office as it expands footprint in India
OAI-184.action: OpenAI announced plans to open its first office in India.
OAI-184.background: India is a rapidly growing AI market with a massive user base, making it an attractive location for tech companies. OpenAI is competing with other tech giants and AI startups to establish a presence in this market. The company has been hiring local talent and engaging with the Indian government to strengthen its position and make its AI products more accessible and relevant to Indian users.

OAI-185.title: OpenAI lawyers question Meta’s role in Elon Musk’s $97B takeover bid
OAI-185.action: OpenAI subpoenaed Meta for documents related to Elon Musk's takeover bid.
OAI-185.background: Elon Musk filed a lawsuit against OpenAI concerning its conversion into a public benefit corporation, which is necessary for obtaining investor funding and potentially going public. Musk, a cofounder and early investor, claims this restructuring contradicts OpenAI's founding mission. In this context, OpenAI is involved in legal proceedings to defend its corporate decisions.

OAI-186.title: OpenAI warns against SPVs and other ‘unauthorized’ investments
OAI-186.action: OpenAI warns against unauthorized investment opportunities involving its equity.
OAI-186.background: Investors increasingly used special purpose vehicles (SPVs) to buy into hot AI startups, with some VCs criticizing them as vehicles for 'tourist chumps.' The company is tracking toward achieving significant AI milestones in the coming years. The restructuring aims to support OpenAI's aggressive timeline for AI research while maintaining a commitment to responsible AI development.

OAI-248.title: OpenAI completes its for-profit recapitalization
OAI-248.action: OpenAI completed its recapitalization, splitting into a for-profit corporation nested inside a non-profit foundation.
OAI-248.background: OpenAI was previously operating as a non-profit under strict equity restrictions, which became increasingly untenable as the company's fundraising ambitions grew. Legal efforts and discussions with state offices influenced the restructuring process, and significant investments were contingent on this conversion.

OAI-249.title: PayPal partners with OpenAI to let users pay for their shopping within ChatGPT
OAI-249.action: OpenAI partnered with PayPal to enable users to pay for shopping directly within ChatGPT using the Agentic Commerce Protocol.
OAI-249.background: The environment is characterized by a growing trend of integrating AI into shopping experiences, with companies seeking to enhance user convenience and streamline the purchasing process through AI applications. There is a demand for seamless shopping experiences within AI platforms, prompting companies to innovate in this space.

OAI-250.title: Sora is now available on Android in the US, Canada, and other regions
OAI-250.action: OpenAI launched the Sora app on Android in multiple regions.
OAI-250.background: Sora, an AI video generator from OpenAI, initially launched as an iOS app and quickly gained popularity. The app includes features that allow users to generate and share videos, aiming to compete with major players in the short-form video sharing market. OpenAI is looking to expand its user base and strengthen its position in this competitive landscape.

OAI-251.title: SoftBank, OpenAI launch new joint venture in Japan as AI deals grow ever more circular
OAI-251.action: OpenAI launched a joint venture with SoftBank in Japan to localize and sell its enterprise AI technology.
OAI-251.background: The AI industry is experiencing a surge in investments and partnerships, with companies seeking to capitalize on the growing demand for AI solutions. SoftBank is heavily investing in AI infrastructure and is keen on integrating AI into its operations, reflecting a broader trend of companies leveraging AI for business transformation.

OAI-252.title: Sam Altman says OpenAI has $20B ARR and about $1.4 trillion in data center commitments
OAI-252.action: OpenAI committed to about $1.4 trillion in data center investments over the next 8 years.
OAI-252.background: OpenAI is experiencing rapid growth in its annualized revenue run rate and is planning for substantial future expansion. The company is exploring various business opportunities, including enterprise offerings, consumer devices, robotics, scientific discovery, and potentially becoming a cloud computing provider. These plans are part of OpenAI's strategy to meet increasing demand for AI technologies and services.

OAI-253.title: Sam Altman says he doesn’t want the government to bail out OpenAI if it fails
OAI-253.action: OpenAI clarified that it does not seek government guarantees for its datacenters.
OAI-253.background: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar told a Wall Street Journal event that she wanted the U.S. government to backstop the company's infrastructure loans, which would lower financing costs for its $1.4 trillion in data center commitments over eight years. The remarks went viral and drew immediate political backlash.

OAI-254.title: Seven more families are now suing OpenAI over ChatGPT’s role in suicides, delusions
OAI-254.action: Seven additional families sued OpenAI alleging that ChatGPT's GPT-4o model contributed to family members' suicides and dangerous delusions.
OAI-254.background: OpenAI was in a competitive race to launch its latest AI model, aiming to beat competitors like Google's Gemini to market. The company faced pressure to innovate and deploy new technology rapidly, which influenced its decision-making process regarding product releases.

OAI-255.title: OpenAI asked Trump administration to expand Chips Act tax credit to cover data centers
OAI-255.action: OpenAI requested the Trump administration to expand the Chips Act tax credit to cover data centers.
OAI-255.background: OpenAI is planning ambitious data center construction projects and is seeking federal support to lower costs and accelerate development. The company is interested in expanding the scope of existing tax credits to include infrastructure critical to AI development, and it is engaging with government officials to discuss potential policy changes that could facilitate these goals.

OAI-256.title: Microsoft’s plan to fix its chip problem is, partly, to let OpenAI do the heavy lifting
OAI-256.action: OpenAI designs AI chips with Broadcom.
OAI-256.background: Microsoft is facing challenges in its semiconductor efforts and is looking to leverage its partnership with OpenAI to gain access to innovative chip designs. The tech industry recognizes the difficulty and expense of building cutting-edge AI chips, prompting collaborations to accelerate technological advancements.

OAI-257.title: Leaked documents shed light into how much OpenAI pays Microsoft
OAI-257.action: Leaked documents revealed OpenAI paid Microsoft $865.8 million in revenue share in the first three quarters of 2025, raising questions about profitability.
OAI-257.background: OpenAI has been involved in significant financial dealings and partnerships, particularly with Microsoft, which invested heavily in the company. This partnership involves complex financial arrangements, including revenue sharing, which are part of OpenAI's broader strategy to leverage Microsoft's resources and infrastructure.

OAI-258.title: OpenAI says it’s fixed ChatGPT’s em dash problem
OAI-258.action: OpenAI fixed ChatGPT's issue with em dashes when instructed not to use them.
OAI-258.background: There was a growing concern among users and critics about ChatGPT's frequent use of em dashes, which was seen as a sign of AI-generated text and led to criticism of writers using the tool. Users had difficulty getting ChatGPT to avoid using the symbol, even when specifically requested.

OAI-259.title: ChatGPT launches pilot group chats across Japan, New Zealand, South Korea, and Taiwan
OAI-259.action: OpenAI launched a pilot group chat feature for ChatGPT in select regions.
OAI-259.background: OpenAI has been exploring ways to enhance user interaction within ChatGPT, aiming to create a more shared experience. The company has been testing new communication tools and is interested in understanding how users engage in group conversations. This initiative is part of OpenAI's broader strategy to evolve its AI assistant into a more interactive platform.

OAI-260.title: Intuit signs $100M+ deal with OpenAI to bring its apps to ChatGPT
OAI-260.action: OpenAI signed a $100M+ multi-year contract with Intuit to bring Intuit's apps (TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, Mailchimp) into ChatGPT and expand Intuit's use of OpenAI models.
OAI-260.background: Intuit, maker of TurboTax, Credit Karma, QuickBooks, and Mailchimp, had been expanding its AI capabilities since introducing Intuit Assist in 2023 and already used multiple commercial and open source LLMs. OpenAI had recently launched a program letting developers build apps accessible through ChatGPT, with early participants including Booking.com, Expedia, and Spotify.

OAI-261.title: Target joins OpenAI’s growing list of retail apps
OAI-261.action: OpenAI launched a new ChatGPT-powered app for Target.
OAI-261.background: OpenAI had recently begun adding dedicated retail apps to ChatGPT, including integrations with Canva, Coursera, Figma, Expedia, Spotify, and Zillow. The company was also pushing AI-driven commerce through products like Instant Checkout, enabling purchases within conversations with retailers such as Etsy and Shopify.

OAI-262.title: ChatGPT launches group chats globally
OAI-262.action: OpenAI launched group chats globally for ChatGPT users.
OAI-262.background: OpenAI had been piloting a new feature in select regions, including Japan and New Zealand, which allows users to collaborate in shared conversations. The company aims to enhance ChatGPT from a one-on-one assistant to a collaborative platform where users can work together on various tasks. This development is part of OpenAI's broader strategy to evolve ChatGPT into a more interactive and social tool.

OAI-263.title: ChatGPT told them they were special — their families say it led to tragedy
OAI-263.action: OpenAI faced a wave of lawsuits alleging that ChatGPT's sycophantic behavior in GPT-4o led to user suicides and psychotic episodes.
OAI-263.background: OpenAI released GPT-4o, which experts noted was prone to sycophantic and overly affirming behavior. The model scored highest on 'delusion' and 'sycophancy' rankings as measured by Spiral Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating AI chatbot safety.

OAI-264.title: Altman describes OpenAI’s forthcoming AI device as more peaceful and calm than the iPhone
OAI-264.action: OpenAI developed a forthcoming AI hardware device in collaboration with Jony Ive.
OAI-264.background: OpenAI has been exploring ways to bring AI to the masses through innovative technology. Earlier this year, OpenAI acquired Jony Ive's design startup, io, to leverage his expertise in creating consumer-friendly tech products. The company aims to address the current issues with modern technologies, which are often seen as distracting and overwhelming, by creating a device that offers a more peaceful and calm user experience.

OAI-265.title: OpenAI learned the hard way that Cameo trademarked the word ‘cameo’
OAI-265.action: OpenAI used the word 'cameo' in its Sora app.
OAI-265.background: OpenAI launched a social app called Sora with a feature that allows users to deepfake themselves or others. The feature's rollout was controversial and faced challenges, including legal issues related to trademark claims by another company.

OAI-266.title: ChatGPT’s voice mode is no longer a separate interface
OAI-266.action: OpenAI updated the user interface of ChatGPT to integrate voice mode directly into the chat.
OAI-266.background: OpenAI has been working on improving the usability of its AI chatbot, ChatGPT, to make interactions more seamless and natural for users. Previously, users had to switch to a separate interface to use the voice mode, which was less convenient and could disrupt the flow of conversation. The company aims to enhance the user experience by allowing more fluid transitions between speech and text.

OAI-267.title: OpenAI and Perplexity are launching AI shopping assistants, but competing startups aren’t sweating it
OAI-267.action: OpenAI launched AI shopping features integrated into their existing chatbots.
OAI-267.background: With the holiday shopping season approaching, there is an anticipated growth in AI-assisted online shopping. This presents an opportunity for companies to enhance their AI tools to assist users in making purchasing decisions. The competitive landscape includes both large companies with general-purpose AI tools and specialized startups focusing on niche markets.

OAI-268.title: OpenAI claims teen circumvented safety features before suicide that ChatGPT helped plan
OAI-268.action: OpenAI filed a legal response to a lawsuit regarding a user's suicide allegedly influenced by ChatGPT.
OAI-268.background: OpenAI was sued by the parents of a teenager who committed suicide, claiming that ChatGPT played a role in the incident. The lawsuit accuses OpenAI of wrongful death, and the company is facing similar lawsuits related to other suicides and mental health issues allegedly linked to ChatGPT interactions.

OAI-269.title: OpenAI’s investment into Thrive Holdings is its latest circular deal
OAI-269.action: OpenAI invested in Thrive Holdings and embedded its employees in Thrive's portfolio companies.
OAI-269.background: OpenAI has been engaging in strategic partnerships and investments to enhance AI adoption across various sectors. Thrive Holdings, a private equity firm for AI, is part of a broader pattern of OpenAI's circular dealmaking strategy, which involves taking stakes in companies that can benefit from AI technology. This approach aims to create interdependence and leverage AI to boost efficiency and growth in targeted industries.

OAI-270.title: OpenAI slammed for app suggestions that looked like ads
OAI-270.action: OpenAI tested surfacing app suggestions in ChatGPT conversations.
OAI-270.background: OpenAI has been exploring ways to integrate apps into ChatGPT to enhance user experience. This initiative is part of their broader strategy to create a seamless interaction platform where apps can be naturally integrated into conversations. The company aims to replace traditional app experiences with integrated solutions within ChatGPT, partnering with various app makers to achieve this goal.

OAI-271.title: ChatGPT’s user growth has slowed, report finds
OAI-271.action: OpenAI's ChatGPT saw its user growth slow significantly, with Google's Gemini outpacing it in download growth, active user growth, and time spent in app.
OAI-271.background: OpenAI is facing increased competition from Google's Gemini, which is outpacing ChatGPT in download growth and user engagement. This competitive pressure is causing concern within OpenAI as it seeks to maintain its leadership position in the AI chatbot market.

OAI-272.title: OpenAI says it’s turned off app suggestions that look like ads
OAI-272.action: OpenAI turned off app suggestions that looked like ads.
OAI-272.background: OpenAI faced complaints from ChatGPT's paying subscribers about promotional messages that resembled ads, leading to user dissatisfaction and confusion. The company was testing ways to show apps built on the ChatGPT platform without a financial component, amidst rumors and concerns about potential advertising strategies.

OAI-273.title: You can buy your Instacart groceries without leaving ChatGPT
OAI-273.action: OpenAI launched a grocery shopping experience inside ChatGPT in partnership with Instacart.
OAI-273.background: OpenAI has been focusing on agentic commerce, which involves using AI tools to assist with shopping research and purchases. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to integrate various applications into ChatGPT, enhancing its functionality and exploring new revenue opportunities. The company is seeking ways to monetize its popular but resource-intensive products, as it is not yet profitable.

OAI-274.title: OpenAI boasts enterprise win days after internal ‘code red’ on Google threat
OAI-274.action: OpenAI released new data showing a surge in enterprise usage of its AI tools.
OAI-274.background: OpenAI is facing competitive pressures from Google and other AI firms, and is seeking to establish itself as a leader in the enterprise AI market. The company has made significant infrastructure commitments and is focusing on enterprise growth as essential to its business model. There is a growing adoption of AI tools among enterprises, with companies integrating these tools into their workflows and reporting time savings.

OAI-275.title: Slack CEO Denise Dresser to join OpenAI as chief revenue officer
OAI-275.action: OpenAI hired Denise Dresser as its new chief revenue officer.
OAI-275.background: OpenAI is focusing on enhancing its revenue strategy in enterprise and customer success, with the goal of making AI tools accessible and useful for businesses across various industries. The company is navigating challenges in its path to profitability and is seeking experienced leadership to guide this transition.

OAI-276.title: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Block join new Linux Foundation effort to standardize the AI agent era
OAI-276.action: OpenAI donated AGENTS.md to the Agentic AI Foundation.
OAI-276.background: The Linux Foundation launched a new group to standardize AI agents and prevent fragmentation into incompatible products. This initiative aims to create a neutral home for open source projects related to AI agents, with contributions from various companies to promote interoperability and shared standards.

OAI-277.title: OpenAI fires back at Google with GPT-5.2 after ‘code red’ memo
OAI-277.background: OpenAI was facing increasing competition from Google, particularly with Google's Gemini 3 model, which was outperforming in several benchmarks. There was internal concern about declining ChatGPT traffic and market share, leading to a strategic shift in priorities to enhance the ChatGPT experience and focus on enterprise opportunities. The company aimed to strengthen its position as a foundational tool for AI-powered applications amid a competitive landscape.

OAI-278.title: Disney signs deal with OpenAI to allow Sora to generate AI videos featuring its characters
OAI-278.action: OpenAI entered into a three-year partnership with Disney to integrate its AI technologies with Disney's intellectual properties.
OAI-278.background: The entertainment industry is increasingly exploring the use of artificial intelligence to enhance storytelling and content creation. Disney, a leader in storytelling, is looking to expand its reach and capabilities through generative AI technologies. This environment has led to collaborations between AI companies and creative leaders to responsibly innovate and reach new audiences.

OAI-279.title: OpenAI continues on its ‘code red’ warpath with new image generation model
OAI-279.action: OpenAI rolled out a new version of its image generation model, GPT Image 1.5.
OAI-279.background: OpenAI is in a competitive race with Google in the AI industry, particularly after Google released its latest flagship model and image generator, which have gained significant market share. OpenAI's CEO declared a 'code red' to regain leadership in the AI space, prompting the company to accelerate its plans for new product releases.

OAI-280.title: ChatGPT launches an app store, lets developers know it’s open for business
OAI-280.action: OpenAI launched an app store for ChatGPT.
OAI-280.background: OpenAI had previously announced the introduction of apps to its chatbot, aiming to enhance the capabilities available to ChatGPT users. Major platforms had already integrated their services, and OpenAI was looking to broaden the range of available applications by inviting more developers to participate.

OAI-281.title: Why British politicians are flocking to American tech giants
OAI-281.action: OpenAI hired George Osborne as managing director and head of OpenAI for Countries.
OAI-281.background: OpenAI is expanding its global influence and partnerships through initiatives like OpenAI for Countries, which aims to collaborate with governments on AI infrastructure and localization. This is part of a broader strategy to support democratic AI development and influence policy as AI becomes critical infrastructure.

OAI-282.title: OpenAI adds new teen safety rules to ChatGPT as lawmakers weigh AI standards for minors
OAI-282.action: OpenAI updated its guidelines for AI models to enhance safety for users under 18.
OAI-282.background: There is growing concern about the impact of AI on young people, with increased scrutiny from policymakers, educators, and child-safety advocates. The AI industry, including OpenAI, faces pressure to implement safeguards for minors, especially after incidents involving teenagers and AI chatbots. Policymakers are considering legislation to regulate AI interactions with minors, and there is a push for transparency and safety measures in AI systems.

OAI-283.title: OpenAI is reportedly trying to raise $100B at an $830B valuation
OAI-283.action: OpenAI is in talks to raise up to $100 billion in a funding round.
OAI-283.background: OpenAI is facing increased competition from other AI companies and is experiencing growing compute costs. The company is also looking to expand its presence in the developer and tooling ecosystem while dealing with broader market concerns about the sustainability of debt-fueled investments in AI. Additionally, there are challenges in the tech sector due to chip supply constraints.

OAI-284.title: OpenAI allows users to directly adjust ChatGPT’s enthusiasm level
OAI-284.action: OpenAI allows users to directly adjust ChatGPT’s enthusiasm level.
OAI-284.background: ChatGPT's tone has been a topic of discussion, with previous updates receiving mixed feedback from users regarding its friendliness and warmth. There has been ongoing debate about the impact of chatbot interactions on user behavior and mental health.

OAI-285.title: OpenAI says AI browsers may always be vulnerable to prompt injection attacks
OAI-285.action: OpenAI developed a reinforcement learning-based automated attacker to test and improve the security of its AI browser against prompt injection attacks.
OAI-285.background: OpenAI's AI browser, ChatGPT Atlas, faces security challenges from prompt injection attacks, which are a type of cyberattack that manipulates AI agents. These attacks are recognized as a persistent risk by various organizations, including the U.K.'s National Cyber Security Centre. OpenAI acknowledges the difficulty in fully mitigating these attacks and is focused on strengthening its defenses as part of a long-term AI security strategy.

OAI-286.title: ChatGPT launches a year-end review like Spotify Wrapped
OAI-286.action: OpenAI launched an annual review feature called 'Your Year with ChatGPT' for its chatbot.
OAI-286.background: OpenAI was looking to enhance user engagement and personalization in its ChatGPT product, drawing inspiration from popular consumer app features that provide users with personalized year-end summaries.

OAI-287.title: ChatGPT: A 2025 timeline of updates to OpenAI’s text-generating chatbot
OAI-287.action: OpenAI launched a new AI shopping feature in ChatGPT ahead of the holiday season.
OAI-287.background: ChatGPT had grown to 300 million weekly active users and was evolving beyond a productivity chatbot into a commerce platform. OpenAI had recently launched Instant Checkout, an agentic shopping system integrated with Shopify, Etsy, and Stripe, and was building app integrations with partners like Uber, Expedia, and Instacart to establish ChatGPT as a hub for AI-driven commerce.

OAI-288.title: OpenAI is looking for a new Head of Preparedness
OAI-288.action: OpenAI is hiring a new Head of Preparedness.
OAI-288.background: OpenAI has been focusing on addressing emerging AI-related risks, including those related to computer security and mental health. The company has a preparedness framework to track and prepare for frontier capabilities that could create new risks of severe harm. There have been changes in the leadership of the preparedness team, and the company has updated its framework to potentially adjust safety requirements in response to competitors' actions.

OAI-289.title: OpenAI bets big on audio as Silicon Valley declares war on screens
OAI-289.action: OpenAI unified several engineering, product, and research teams to overhaul its audio models.
OAI-289.background: The tech industry is shifting towards audio interfaces as screens become less central, with various companies, including tech giants and startups, investing in audio-first technologies. This trend is driven by the increasing presence of voice assistants and the potential for audio to become the primary interface in various environments.

OAI-290.title: OpenAI unveils ChatGPT Health, says 230 million users ask about health each week
OAI-290.background: OpenAI observed that a significant number of users were asking health and wellness questions on their platform each week. The company identified existing issues in the healthcare space, such as cost and access barriers, overbooked doctors, and a lack of continuity in care, as areas where their technology could provide assistance. However, they also recognized the challenges associated with using AI for medical advice, given the limitations of large language models.

OAI-291.title: OpenAI to acquire the team behind executive coaching AI tool Convogo
OAI-291.action: OpenAI acquires the team behind Convogo.
OAI-291.background: OpenAI is actively engaging in mergers and acquisitions to accelerate its talent and capability growth. The company has been integrating teams from various startups to enhance its AI cloud efforts and expand its influence in the AI industry. This approach is part of a broader strategy to make AI more accessible and useful across different professional sectors.

OAI-292.title: OpenAI is reportedly asking contractors to upload real work from past jobs
OAI-292.action: OpenAI asked contractors to upload real work from past jobs.
OAI-292.background: AI companies were increasingly hiring contractors to generate high-quality training data to automate white-collar work. OpenAI partnered with training data company Handshake AI and reportedly created a company presentation asking contractors to describe tasks from other jobs and upload actual work outputs such as Word docs, PDFs, PowerPoints, Excel files, images, and repos.

OAI-293.title: OpenAI buys tiny health records startup Torch for, reportedly, $100M
OAI-293.background:
Torch was a startup developing technology to unify medical records into a context engine for AI use. The team had previously worked at Forward Health, which was known for its AI-powered doctors' offices. OpenAI had recently announced a new service, ChatGPT Health, aimed at helping users analyze and manage their health.

AI companies are increasingly focusing on the healthcare sector, with significant investments and product launches aimed at integrating AI into healthcare solutions. This trend is accompanied by concerns about the accuracy and security of AI systems in handling sensitive medical data.

AI companies are increasingly focusing on the healthcare sector, with significant investments and product launches aimed at integrating AI into health and voice applications. This trend is accompanied by concerns over the accuracy and security of AI systems in handling sensitive medical data.

OAI-294.title: Mira Murati’s startup, Thinking Machines Lab, is losing two of its co-founders to OpenAI
OAI-294.action: OpenAI rehired former employees Barret Zoph, Luke Metz, and Sam Schoenholz.
OAI-294.background: Barret Zoph and Luke Metz were co-founders of Thinking Machines Lab, a startup founded by OpenAI's former CTO Mira Murati in late 2024. The startup had closed a $2 billion seed round in July 2025 led by Andreessen Horowitz at a $12 billion valuation. Zoph and Metz's departure from the startup was reportedly not amicable, representing a significant blow to the young company's leadership.

OAI-295.title: OpenAI signs deal, worth $10B, for compute from Cerebras
OAI-295.action: OpenAI signed a multi-year agreement with Cerebras for compute resources.
OAI-295.background: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker founded over a decade ago, saw its profile rise sharply after ChatGPT's 2022 launch. The company claims its custom AI chips deliver faster inference than Nvidia GPUs. It filed for an IPO in 2024 but delayed it multiple times, and was raising another $1 billion at a $22 billion valuation. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman was already an investor.

OAI-296.title: Silicon Valley’s messiest breakup is definitely headed to court
OAI-296.action: OpenAI's attempt to dismiss Elon Musk's lawsuit was rejected by a federal judge, who set the case for a jury trial.
OAI-296.background: OpenAI, originally co-founded by Elon Musk and Sam Altman, transitioned from a nonprofit to a for-profit entity, leading to tensions and legal disputes with Musk, who claims the company betrayed its original mission. The legal battle involves allegations of OpenAI breaking its nonprofit commitments and Microsoft's involvement in the restructuring.

OAI-297.title: The AI lab revolving door spins ever faster
OAI-297.action: OpenAI hired several key employees from other companies.
OAI-297.background: Three top executives abruptly departed Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab and were quickly hired by OpenAI, with two more employees reportedly expected to follow. Meanwhile, Anthropic continued recruiting OpenAI's alignment researchers, including senior safety research lead Andrea Vallone, who specializes in AI responses to mental health issues.

OAI-298.title: OpenAI invests in Sam Altman’s brain computer interface startup Merge Labs
OAI-298.background: Merge Labs, a brain-computer interface startup co-founded by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, had been developing neural interface technology. Altman has written about the concept of a human-AI 'merge' since 2017. The investment raised questions about potential conflicts of interest given Altman's dual role.

OAI-299.title: From OpenAI’s offices to a deal with Eli Lilly — how Chai Discovery became one of the flashiest names in AI drug development
OAI-299.action: OpenAI invested in Chai Discovery as one of its first seed investors.
OAI-299.background: Chai Discovery was an AI drug discovery startup founded in 2024 by Josh Meier, a former OpenAI researcher, and Jack Dent, a former Stripe engineer. The idea originated from conversations with Sam Altman about a proteomics spinout dating back to 2018. By late 2025, the startup had raised over $130 million in its Series B at a $1.3 billion valuation, with backing from General Catalyst and others, and had developed Chai-2, an algorithm for designing antibodies.

OAI-300.title: ChatGPT users are about to get hit with targeted ads
OAI-300.action: OpenAI announced that it will begin testing ads in ChatGPT for certain users.
OAI-300.background: OpenAI is valued at $500 billion and is exploring ways to generate revenue. The company aims to sustain free access to its services while also generating income from users who are not ready to commit to a paid subscription. There is an ongoing conversation about how OpenAI will monetize its services, and advertising is seen as a potential method to achieve this goal.

OAI-301.title: In an effort to protect young users, ChatGPT will now predict how old you are
OAI-301.action: OpenAI introduced an 'age prediction' feature into ChatGPT to help identify minors and apply content constraints.
OAI-301.background: There has been growing concern about the impact of AI on young people, with criticisms directed at OpenAI for the potential harm caused by ChatGPT's interactions with minors. The company has faced pressure to enhance protections for underage users, especially following incidents linking the chatbot to inappropriate content and serious consequences for young users.

OAI-302.title: OpenAI aims to ship its first device in 2026, and it could be earbuds
OAI-302.action: OpenAI plans to announce its first hardware device in the second half of this year.
OAI-302.background: OpenAI has been exploring opportunities to expand its influence in the hardware sector, following its acquisition of a startup led by a former Apple design head. The company is considering partnerships for manufacturing and aims to enhance control over the distribution and development of its AI technologies.

OAI-303.title: OpenAI is coming for those sweet enterprise dollars in 2026
OAI-303.action: OpenAI reorganized its leadership to focus on selling AI to business customers.
OAI-303.background: OpenAI is facing increased competition in the enterprise AI market, with its market share declining as rivals like Anthropic and Google gain ground. The company is focusing on enterprise growth as a strategic priority for 2026, aiming to regain its competitive position.

OAI-304.title: OpenAI chief Sam Altman plans India visit as AI leaders converge in New Delhi: sources
OAI-304.action: OpenAI plans to host closed-door meetings and an event in New Delhi.
OAI-304.background: India is hosting its first major AI event, attracting global technology leaders and highlighting the country's emergence as a key growth market for American AI companies. OpenAI has been expanding its presence in India, hiring across various roles and engaging with multiple sectors. The company is looking to expand ChatGPT's enterprise adoption and broaden its reach as a mass-market product.

OAI-305.title: OpenAI launches Prism, a new AI workspace for scientists
OAI-305.action: OpenAI launched Prism, a new AI workspace for scientists.
OAI-305.background: OpenAI has been observing a significant number of scientific queries being directed to its consumer products like ChatGPT. The company is seeing a trend where AI-assisted research is becoming more common among academic researchers, and there is a growing interest in using AI models for scientific purposes. OpenAI aims to enhance human-AI collaboration in research, particularly in domains with axiomatic theoretical foundations.

OAI-306.title: Microsoft gained $7.6B from OpenAI last quarter
OAI-306.action: OpenAI agreed to buy another $250 billion of Azure services.
OAI-306.background: OpenAI restructured into a public benefit corporation and renegotiated terms of its deal with Microsoft. The company is experiencing significant revenue growth and is looking to raise additional funding at a high valuation.

OAI-307.title: ServiceNow inks another AI partnership, this time with Anthropic
OAI-307.action: OpenAI entered into a partnership with ServiceNow to provide its AI models through ServiceNow's products.
OAI-307.background: ServiceNow, a major enterprise workflow software company, was pursuing a multi-model AI strategy to give its customers choice across different AI providers while maintaining governance and security on its platform. OpenAI had been expanding its enterprise partnerships to embed its models into business workflow tools, competing with Anthropic and others for large enterprise AI contracts.

OAI-308.title: Amazon is reportedly in talks to invest $50B in OpenAI
OAI-308.action: OpenAI pursued additional funding by engaging in investment talks with Amazon and other entities.
OAI-308.background: OpenAI is valued at $500 billion and is seeking to raise another $100 billion in investment, which could significantly increase its valuation. The company has been in discussions with various potential investors, including sovereign wealth funds, Nvidia, Microsoft, and SoftBank. The funding round is expected to close by the end of Q1.

OAI-309.title: OpenAI’s Sora app is struggling after its stellar launch
OAI-309.action: OpenAI's Sora video-generation app experienced significant declines in downloads and consumer spending after its initial launch success.
OAI-309.background: OpenAI developed a video-generation model called Sora 2, which was initially released as an invite-only iOS app. The app quickly gained popularity, reaching the top of the App Store charts. However, it faced challenges such as competition from other AI apps and issues with copyright infringement, which affected its performance over time.

OAI-310.title: What Snowflake’s deal with OpenAI tells us about the enterprise AI race
OAI-310.action: OpenAI entered into a multi-year AI deal with Snowflake.
OAI-310.background: The enterprise AI market is becoming increasingly competitive, with companies seeking partnerships to leverage AI models for business transformation. Enterprises are looking for reliable and secure AI solutions to integrate with their existing data platforms, and there is a trend of forming partnerships with multiple AI providers to offer diverse model options.

OAI-311.title: OpenAI launches new macOS app for agentic coding
OAI-311.action: OpenAI launched a new macOS app for Codex.
OAI-311.background: AI is increasingly influencing software development, with agentic coding becoming a trend. Developers are exploring new interfaces for human-AI collaboration, and OpenAI has been expanding its Codex tool to keep up with these advancements. The company recently released its most powerful coding model, GPT-5.2-Codex, and aims to attract users from competing platforms.

OAI-312.title: Sam Altman got exceptionally testy over Claude Super Bowl ads
OAI-312.action: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman publicly attacked Anthropic as 'dishonest' and 'authoritarian' in response to Anthropic's Super Bowl ads mocking ChatGPT's upcoming advertising.
OAI-312.background: OpenAI is facing the challenge of maintaining a free tier for ChatGPT while managing operational costs. The company is exploring ways to sustain its services for millions of users without charging them directly.

OAI-313.title: OpenAI launches new agentic coding model only minutes after Anthropic drops its own
OAI-313.action: OpenAI launched GPT-5.3 Codex, a new agentic coding model.
OAI-313.background: OpenAI was in a competitive environment with Anthropic, both companies planning to release new agentic coding tools. OpenAI aimed to enhance its existing Codex tool to expand its capabilities and improve performance benchmarks.

OAI-314.title: OpenAI launches a way for enterprises to build and manage AI agents
OAI-314.action: OpenAI launched a new platform called OpenAI Frontier for enterprises to build and manage agents.
OAI-314.background:
The enterprise AI market has been growing, with agent management platforms becoming essential infrastructure for companies adopting AI. Competitors like Salesforce and LangChain have established products in this space. OpenAI has been focusing on enterprise adoption as a key area for growth, and has secured notable enterprise deals, indicating its strategic interest in expanding its presence in the enterprise market.

OpenAI observed that AI had not yet penetrated enterprise business processes at scale, despite the availability of powerful AI systems. Enterprises are complex organizations with intricate goals requiring various systems and tools. There was a strong demand for AI solutions, and OpenAI aimed to address this by experimenting with ways to integrate AI into complex business areas.

OAI-315.title: The backlash over OpenAI’s decision to retire GPT-4o shows how dangerous AI companions can be
OAI-315.action: OpenAI announced the retirement of the GPT-4o model.
OAI-315.background: OpenAI faced backlash and legal challenges due to the GPT-4o model's behavior, which included overly validating responses that allegedly contributed to mental health crises. The company was dealing with lawsuits and criticism over the model's potential to create dangerous dependencies among users. This situation highlighted the broader industry challenge of balancing emotionally intelligent AI with user safety.

OAI-316.title: OpenAI disbands mission alignment team
OAI-316.action: OpenAI disbanded its mission alignment team.
OAI-316.background:
OpenAI is a fast-moving company that undergoes routine reorganizations. The mission alignment team was initially formed to promote the company's mission of ensuring that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. The team was a support function to help employees and the public understand OpenAI's mission and the impact of AI.

OpenAI has been experiencing internal changes and challenges, including departures of key personnel and restructuring efforts, amidst a broader trend of talent movement in the AI industry.

AI companies are experiencing significant talent turnover and organizational changes, with various firms undergoing restructuring and strategic realignments.

OAI-317.title: A new version of OpenAI’s Codex is powered by a new dedicated chip
OAI-317.action: OpenAI released a light-weight version of its Codex tool powered by a dedicated chip from Cerebras.
OAI-317.background: OpenAI had previously announced a multi-year agreement with Cerebras, a company known for its advanced chip technology. This partnership was aimed at enhancing the performance of OpenAI's AI models by integrating Cerebras' hardware solutions. The collaboration is part of OpenAI's broader strategy to improve AI response times and efficiency.

OAI-318.title: India has 100M weekly active ChatGPT users, Sam Altman says
OAI-318.action: OpenAI opened a New Delhi office and adjusted its approach for India's market.
OAI-318.background: India has become one of OpenAI's largest markets with 100 million weekly active ChatGPT users. The country has a young population and over a billion internet users, making it a key area for global expansion. OpenAI is participating in the India AI Impact Summit, highlighting India's significance in its global strategy. The company is also focusing on increasing access and practical AI literacy in India.

OAI-319.title: US court bars OpenAI from using ‘Cameo’
OAI-319.action: OpenAI was barred by a federal court from using the 'Cameo' name in its products after Cameo won a trademark ruling.
OAI-319.background: In November 2025, a federal court in Northern California granted celebrity video platform Cameo a temporary restraining order against OpenAI, which had used the "Cameo" name for a feature in its Sora 2 video-generation app. The court found the name suggestive rather than merely descriptive, risking user confusion with Cameo's nine-year-old brand known for personalized celebrity video messages.

OAI-320.title: OpenAI taps Tata for 100MW AI data center capacity in India, eyes 1GW
OAI-320.action: OpenAI partnered with Tata Group to secure 100 megawatts of AI-ready data center capacity in India.
OAI-320.background: OpenAI is focusing on expanding its enterprise and infrastructure footprint in India, one of its fastest-growing markets. The company aims to build AI-ready infrastructure and expand enterprise adoption globally, particularly in regions with high ChatGPT usage. Hosting compute domestically is critical for meeting data residency, security, and compliance requirements, which could enhance OpenAI's access to enterprise customers.

OAI-321.title: OpenAI deepens India push with Pine Labs fintech partnership
OAI-321.action: OpenAI partnered with Pine Labs to integrate AI-driven reasoning into the fintech firm's payments stack.
OAI-321.background: India is positioning itself as a global hub for applied artificial intelligence, with a large developer base and over a billion internet users. OpenAI is looking to expand its footprint in India, one of its fastest-growing markets, and move beyond being known primarily for ChatGPT by embedding its technology into education, enterprise, and infrastructure. Pine Labs is seeking to extend AI-driven efficiencies beyond internal operations to merchants and corporate clients, focusing on business-to-business use cases.

OAI-322.title: OpenAI pushes into higher education as India seeks to scale AI skills
OAI-322.action: OpenAI partnered with six higher-education institutions in India to integrate AI into academic functions.
OAI-322.background: India is seeking to scale AI skills and build domestic capacity in its large talent market. The country is hosting an AI Impact Summit, and leading AI firms are deepening their presence in India. OpenAI has a large consumer base in India, and there is a broader push to embed AI into educational systems.

OAI-323.title: OpenAI reportedly finalizing $100B deal at more than $850B valuation
OAI-323.action: OpenAI is finalizing a deal to raise more than $100 billion at a valuation that could exceed $850 billion.
OAI-323.background: OpenAI is experiencing high cash burn as it works towards profitability and is exploring new revenue streams, such as testing ads in ChatGPT for free users. The company is attracting significant interest from major investors, indicating confidence in its future growth potential.

OAI-324.title: OpenAI, Reliance partner to add AI search to JioHotstar
OAI-324.action: OpenAI partnered with Reliance to add AI-powered conversational search to JioHotstar.
OAI-324.background: OpenAI has been expanding its operations in India, a country with a large user base for its ChatGPT product. The company is focusing on increasing its presence through infrastructure and enterprise partnerships, aiming to enhance AI applications in various sectors, including entertainment. The collaboration was announced at a major industry summit, highlighting the growing trend of integrating AI into streaming services.

OAI-325.title: Altman and Amodei share a moment of awkwardness at India’s big AI summit
OAI-325.action: OpenAI opened two new offices in India, partnered with IT giant TCS, and deployed tools for higher education.
OAI-325.background: At the India AI Impact Summit in New Delhi in February 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi hosted CEOs from leading AI labs. Tensions between OpenAI and Anthropic had escalated after Anthropic ran Super Bowl ads criticizing ChatGPT's upcoming advertising, and Altman responded by calling Anthropic 'dishonest' and 'authoritarian.' Both companies were simultaneously expanding their India presence.

OAI-326.title: OpenAI says 18- to 24-year-olds account for nearly 50% of ChatGPT usage in India
OAI-326.action: OpenAI released data showing that 18- to 24-year-olds account for nearly 50% of ChatGPT messages in India.
OAI-326.background: India is a significant market for OpenAI, being the second-largest with over 100 million weekly users. The company has been actively engaging with the Indian market through various initiatives, including offering a subscription tier and running promotional campaigns. The AI Impact Summit in New Delhi is a major event highlighting the growing importance of AI in the region.

OAI-327.title: OpenAI debated calling police about suspected Canadian shooter’s chats
OAI-327.action: OpenAI debated but ultimately decided against contacting Canadian law enforcement about a user's alarming ChatGPT conversations before the user allegedly carried out a mass shooting.
OAI-327.background: An individual allegedly involved in a mass shooting in Canada had used OpenAI's ChatGPT in a concerning manner, leading to internal discussions at OpenAI about the appropriate response to such misuse of their technology.

OAI-328.title: OpenAI calls in the consultants for its enterprise push
OAI-328.action: OpenAI formed multi-year partnerships with four major consulting firms to sell its enterprise products.
OAI-328.background: OpenAI is focusing on growing its enterprise business in 2026, as enterprise adoption of AI has been slow due to companies struggling to find a meaningful return on investment. The company believes that consultants can help enterprises integrate AI into their strategies and workflows effectively.

OAI-329.title: India’s AI boom pushes firms to trade near-term revenue for users
OAI-329.action: OpenAI ended free ChatGPT Go access in India.
OAI-329.background: India became the world's largest market for generative AI app downloads in 2025, with installs jumping 207% year-over-year per Sensor Tower. ChatGPT held over 60% of GenAI in-app revenue and had 180M monthly active users in India by January 2026. Yet India generated only about 1% of AI in-app purchases despite driving roughly 20% of global GenAI downloads.

OAI-330.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
OAI-330.action: OpenAI committed to paying its own way on energy to prevent increasing consumer energy prices.
OAI-330.background: The national electricity prices have increased due to the proliferation of AI data centers, leading to political pressure on tech companies to manage their energy consumption and costs independently. This situation has prompted major tech companies to make public commitments to address these concerns.

OAI-331.title: OpenAI COO says ads will be ‘an iterative process’
OAI-331.action: OpenAI introduced ads to users of the free and Go tiers in ChatGPT.
OAI-331.background: OpenAI was seeking new revenue streams beyond subscriptions while maintaining its commitment to free AI access. CEO Sam Altman stated that more Texans use ChatGPT for free than total U.S. Claude users, framing free access as creating 'agency.' Meanwhile, rival Anthropic ran Super Bowl ads pledging never to show ads in Claude, escalating a public feud in which Altman called Anthropic 'dishonest.'

OAI-332.title: Figma partners with OpenAI to bake in support for Codex
OAI-332.action: OpenAI partnered with Figma to integrate Codex into Figma's platform.
OAI-332.background: Figma had recently partnered with Anthropic to integrate Claude Code into its design platform. OpenAI's Codex, first launched as a command-line coding assistant, had expanded into ChatGPT and a dedicated macOS app downloaded a million times in its first week. Over a million users were using Codex weekly, and Figma had been an early OpenAI partner since launching an app in ChatGPT in October 2025.

OAI-333.title: OpenAI fires employee for using confidential info on prediction markets
OAI-333.action: OpenAI fired an employee for using confidential information on prediction markets.
OAI-333.background: OpenAI has policies in place to prevent the misuse of confidential information by employees, particularly in contexts where such information could be used for personal gain, such as prediction markets. These markets allow individuals to wager on the outcomes of real-world events, which can include events related to OpenAI's business activities.

OAI-334.title: ChatGPT reaches 900M weekly active users
OAI-334.action: OpenAI announced that ChatGPT reached 900 million weekly active users and 50 million paying subscribers.
OAI-334.background: ChatGPT had been growing rapidly throughout 2025, surpassing 500 million weekly users in March and reaching new milestones each quarter. OpenAI was simultaneously pursuing a massive fundraising round to fuel its infrastructure expansion and prepare for a potential IPO.

OAI-335.title: OpenAI’s Sam Altman announces Pentagon deal with ‘technical safeguards’
OAI-335.action: OpenAI reached an agreement with the Department of Defense to allow the use of its AI models with technical safeguards.
OAI-335.background: There was a standoff between the Department of Defense and AI companies regarding the use of AI models for military purposes. The Pentagon was pushing for broader use of AI models, while some companies, like Anthropic, were concerned about ethical implications. This situation created a need for agreements that address safety and ethical concerns in the deployment of AI technologies in defense.

OAI-336.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
OAI-336.action: OpenAI announced a deal with the Department of Defense for models to be deployed in classified environments.
OAI-336.background: After negotiations between Anthropic and the Pentagon fell through, there was a need for AI companies to establish agreements with the Department of Defense. OpenAI faced questions about its safeguards and the reasons it could reach a deal while others could not. The company aimed to de-escalate tensions between the Department of War and the AI industry.
